I usually wear a 14/16 or 0x/1x.  I am 38 E/F.  I always purchase the 3x in this sweater because it is a pretty snug fit.  I own several colors of this sweater because it works so well.  They wash and dry nicely.  I delicate/handwash in my machine and then lay flat in my dryer closet.  The colors are nice and dark (like navy is a true navy and black is black not gray).  Sleeves are long but can be scrunched.

I am 5'7" - 38 E/F - I usually wear a 0x or 14/16.  I sized up to a 2x because I wanted to able to wash and dry.  It was the right choice - fits well, a little loosely.  I haven't washed up, but I will update my review afterwards if the info would be useful.  It is a thin... not exactly see through, but it's thin.  The color is good - I chose the black/white.  The white is bright white - the black is dark.  The buttons are plastic and white.  It will be a great option for travel/packing/wearing as layer, etc.  For the money I think it's a good tshirt.  I would recommend sizing up if you have heavier bust line (I don't like a shirt that is too stretched over my bust area).

I'd give these 10 stars if I could - it seems simple, but it's the best thing I've used for slip on access to tie on shoes.  I've tried super strong magnet clasps (SUPER hard to to adjust - need a second person while you're standing in shoes!).  Tried the corkscrew elastic laces (did NOT lace - just knotted up). And then I found these watching shark tank.  I did make a mistake and order my first set too long (NOTE: measure the laces you want to replace) - so I ordered 2 pairs of the right size.  I'm looking at ALL my lacing shoes to determine how many more I need and what colors.  Amazon is a little lacking in selection but you can buy them directly.  I honestly love these.  They are SUPER easy to adjust unlike all the others I have tried. It takes just a minute or two to hit the sweet spot on the adjustment - tightness to wear and the right amount of give to slip on - and then you're off!  It seems so easy that it couldn't possibly be good, but you've gotta try to believe it.  I only bought 1 pair because I had been so disappointed with others.  I just couldn't be happier.  It's so cool to slip on shoes and go!

I am 5'7", 38 E/F, usually wear a 14/16 or 0x/1x.  I purchased largest size I could because I don't like things tight over my bust.  The navy is dark.  Washed and dried (on hand wash cycle and no heat, laid flat dryer closet) well.  It is a shrug, so it's short bodied.  I like it. The material is soft and comfortable.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

I am 5'7"... 38E/F and usually wear a 14/16 or 0x/1x.  I purchased the 2x and 3x because I wanted a really loose fit and was HOPING it would come closer together over my bust area.  No such luck - it doesn't even make it half way across.  It's just not going to work the way I had hoped.  It is a lighter navy which was also disappointing.  I was happy for it to be a nylon like material - for the draping- but it will snag easily.  It washed and dried (on hand wash cycle and laid flat in dryer closet) - but was still wrinkly - eh.  I wanted it to work, but it just will not.

Really cute metallic gold coin purse.  Has a key ring inside the main pouch.  Big enough to put credit card into.  No horrible smells... I've had bad luck there lately...  Really pretty - but I like metallic gold.  :)  Not sure if it is leather or pleather - but either way it feels like it will last a while.  Time will tell.  Having said that, I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

Very cute little coin purse... the color represented in the ad is pretty darn close to what it is too - a mulberry?  It's small and has zippers with little heart pulls.  No horrible smells either.  I've had bad luck with that lately.

Says that it is black leather... might be.  The closure is a silver colored material that is sharp in certain areas - which is disappointing.  But the worst is the SMELL.  It is horrible.  I've put it out in the sun for 2 days, used Febreeze and nothing can cut the smell.  It's an old musty smell. I'm afraid anything coming near it is going to pick up the stink. Horrible.  So - in the trash it goes - not worth the effort to return.

These bunny slippers are SUPER SUPER soft fur and cozy.  Very cute - however, they are a little short.  I ordered the size 8-9 - it's more like a 7 1/2- 8.  That part was a little disappointing.  I purchased 2, the white and grey.  One pair for my Mom who is a 9.  I am more like an 8 1/2.  Works for me, but I worry that it will be a bit short for my mom - but I purchased them to wear on Easter day - so maybe we can get away with it for the day.
These socks are super soft and cozy - a little thick, but a nice Easter day wear in my opinion.  The little bunnies have a little fuzzy aspect that makes them stand out from the sock just a little.

I purchased the longer 20" strand and this strand is a bit shorter than that and fits more like an 18" - and that is ok... super polishy... round beads. Twist closure.  The necklace is NOT knotted between each bead... They actually look and feel like plastic beads but does come with a certificate of "authenticity"... if you believe it. I hope they are all natural amber.  I have come to find that I prefer the more natural bead and unpolished amber beads - but this is a pretty necklace.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

The color is lighter than I expected and I was really disappointed at the cheap cotton elastic.  Including the knot that shows in the photo I have provided.  Eh.  The beads are roundish.. and unpolished as advertised.  II prefer the more natural look of the unpolished amber. I have an average sized wrist and the bracelets (I purchased 2 of these) are so tight that they leave marks when I removed them.  I imagine the cheap cotton elastic will loosen with age.  I just wonder how long before the elastic breaks.  Eh. All that said, I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

Unpolished, roundish amber beaded bracelet with gradient colors.  Amber is very light and this bracelet is very light.  I am disappointed by the cheap elastic band they are threaded on... It will not last for long.  I guess you what you pay for.. but I was hoping for a bit better quality than a cotton elastic.  I have an average size wrist but the 7" is snug.  I think that as I wear it the cotton elastic is going to loosen before it breaks - so that may be part of their plan for fit.  It's tight enough that it leaves marks after I remove the bracelet.  The gradient colors are beautiful.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

I love that between the amber beads, it is all individually knotted.  It has a twist on clasp.  Easy to put on and take off.  It is a bit short and not actually an 18" fit... but it's not a choker either.  I purchased it because I think amber is beautiful, and I am hopeful there may be some truth to the natural aspect of pain relief.  We shall see.  It is a beautiful piece, each bead is very unique and beautiful.  I purchased a round bead necklace version and I have found that is one is favorite.  I like the random, more natural pieces of this necklace.  The natural beads are polished, but not overly so.  I have included a photo to show what I have.  Despite being a little shorter than indicated, it still fits nicely on my skin.  I am very, very happy with this purchase. I actually purchased one for my mom who is 82 as well.  We're hoping for some magic, but even if it's "just a necklace" it's beautiful.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

Horribly disappointed.  I ignored other reviews that report the same as I am.  Completely crushed, unrecoverable.  I did attempt to steam correct, no luck.  Arrived with one thin piece of brown paper that I assume was supposed to be inside the hat, it was outside the hat further crushing the design - particularly the side flower area.  No way to recover it.  Colors are off from the photos, but I was expecting that - they are never exact.  The pink is hot the orange less so - I was sort of hoping for more of a coral but it's flat orange.  All in all it ends up looking like a $1.99 costume hat.  I was thinking that the barrel crush was just the packing, however, it appears the sewing is part of the problem and it is actually lower one side!  I've said it before - but it's junk.  It needed to packed in a box and properly paper stuffed in order to be viable.  I wish I would not have ignored other reviews reporting their experience with crushed items.  Went right into the trash.  Hoping to save others the disappointment.  I have also provided photos to prove my thoughts.
Very thin, as advertised.  Mock neck.  Suitable for layering.  Machine washed and dried without issue.  Sleeves were a little shorter than I like.

Pretty close to size.  It's pretty thin material, but I like my turtle/mocks to be thinner as they are usually going under something.  The pink is what I would call a hot pink - but not neon.  Pretty close to photo in my opinion.  Machine wash tumble dry.  All in all, it's a useful layer piece.

Woo hoo.  This IS a feather boa.  It's huge and fluffy.  I took it outside and gently shook it a bit.  Lots and lots of dust and tiny feathers, plus a few large ones.  This should be expected.  It is a lovely coral pink.  I purchased as part of costume so it won't be used a lot, but it looks rich and heavy.  Overall, and in the context of my costume, it's perfect.

First, sizing... it appears to be SUPER SUPER SMALL... asian sizing?? The 3x fits like a large. Eh. I am 38E/F. There is NO way I can wear as a button up. Second, the smell! It has the most horrible petroleum (?) odor. I have no idea what it is but the chemical smell is overwhelming. I am not keeping it so I won't comment on washing. Third, it arrived rolled up in a clear plastic bag with NO tags. Eh.
It contains some portion of spandex and I think polyester of some sort. ? But who knows. What a shame. It's just junk.
The best part? The only positive thing I can say is the color - it is listed as "pink" but it is the perfect coral color. I really, really wanted it to work. I LOVED the color. But sadly the color alone could not "save" it from the garbage bin. Live ... and learn. We hope.

I really wanted to LOVE these... really glad I only purchased one to try first. They are very difficult to set. I spent over an hour to "adjust" just one side! The magnets are very strong - good and frustrating. * make sure you keep your two shoes apart while you adjusting because the magnets will marry to the closest magnet. Another hint, set them WAY WAY looser than you think you need. It's easier to tighten in adjustment than to loosen. I initially tried one piece on a different pair of sneakers and because of how the tongue laid, I couldn't use these. So... unlace the magnet and start all over. OH NO! After going through this I feel that having a second person help while you have your shoes on (standing up so you can get the right tight on) would be best in my opinion. They say - you can take them and reuse them over and over again. I want to cry when I think about having to move to another pair! Which brings me to the price. YIKES. I think it would be less disappointing at about 1/2 the price - currently it adds $20 to price of your pair of shoes. So, as long as the pair of shoes I have these currently set on have soles, those magnets are ON. I didn't cut the laces off as suggested just in case I decided NOT to continue - but in all reality laces are fairly cheap. On the other hand, waste not want not. I purchased the white to go on some NB black and white sneakers. It "looks" ok. As for the fit... I am not able to get that nice snug lace fit. The other part of this is that if you try to get them really snug, like you would laces, they will pop right open as that is the magnet's clue you're trying to take them off. You do still need to bend over and attach them together - in case that is an issue for you. And as I said before I highly recommend having a second person help to get the perfect fit. I am so tired of tightening.. OH NO too tight. Readjust... OH NO it's too loose. Over and over again. Having said all that, I can't really recommend these. Sort of like trying to build a better mouse trap I think. I've tried so many of the "no tie" options. So far, this set is one of my least favs due to the very frustrating installation that I can't quite perfect the fit without a second set of hands. Eh. The video makes is look like a breeze, but in my experience, quite a bit more difficult than shown.
UPDATE 5/15/2021 I just purchased something I can't believe is so easy Stretch laces. Works like I had HOPED these would! Tie as you like and leave it tied. They give just a enough to slip them on. NO MORE magnets that snap to each other... no way to comfortably adjust to the right tightness on your foot... you don't need a second person to help adjust to that comfort level... I just tossed the magnets. So glad I only bought one pair.

Husband is a big guy and needed these to wear under work gear - sort of like long johns in a way.  They work perfectly.  They wash and dry wonderfully.  The are soft and a loose fit - even for big guys.  All in all my husband loves them.

I was disappointed in the cheap feel of the fabric, but you get what you pay for.  The fit is VERY tight, so if you're my size of chest, their biggest size is too snug for my comfort (Me: 38E/F)  It is stretchy, but that makes it look even more snug in my opinion... I was rolling the dice I know... but it is not a top I would recommend unless you have a smaller chest area.  The curly edges of the neck and sleeves are not nearly as defined as shown in the photo, which, in my opinion, adds to cheap look.

I purchased this bag for a friend who is a really big butterfly fan. I am using it like gift wrap - putting her gifts inside of this reusable bag.  The bag itself is a sort of nylon material.. The print is black background with purple and bluish butterflies. the interior is black.. there is a zipper closure.  The straps are a black nylon or like material. She's going to love it in my opinion.  I love minimizing waste (gift wrap) too. I was able to fit a jar about (5" tall and about 3" in diameter) and a box about 8x8"  in with room to spare.  Love it - and pretty sure my friend will too.

I purchased this for my 92 year old Mom.  She loves it.  As I am not allowed to see my Mom during this time of virus (she lives in an independent living center that is VERY serious about stopping virus spread) - I haven't seen it on her, but she says it is cute and useful.  Good enough for me!

I purchased this a size up and it is still really too snug across the bust.  Sorta gives you a very top heavy look - which I hate.  I will be donating it as it is not worth returning.  Very disappointed.  It looks like it may have been the last one.  It is a very dirty ivory - so beigy ivory (?) The dots are fuzzy.  The neck doesn't fold because the print is on the outside. So you have to wear it sort of loosy goosey - which isn't awful but I would have liked to loosed fold it.  Overall, not Nike's best effort - especially in sizing.  I have dozens of their women's tops and they usually all fit the same.  I am 5'7" usually wear a 14/16 or ox/1x - 38DDD.  The other thing I noticed is that it is very short.  It's not crop - but BARELY long enough.  Eh.

The duffle is a soft pink duffle.  I was going to use it store switch game accessories in it.. but it's really way too big for the place I wanted to store it.  So, I did donate it because it was just too much hassle to return (vs the price).  The material is thick and it looked like it would have lasted for a long time.  I am sure someone is going to be really excited about it.

This type of eye glass holder is not really a new idea - but it is my current favorite for hanging my readers on.  I've been trying lots of options.. the necklaces - cloth - braided - beaded.  I am NOT a fan.  This particular version is slightly different than the single magnet style - but it's very subtle when on.  The magnets are exceptionally strong on this style - maybe because there are 2.  The butterflies are a gold colored metal and are pretty.  I have to admit I thought they were flowers when I ordered them. HA. But I like the butterflies.  I have tried on many different thicknesses and types (slippery chiffon/satin - cotton tshirts - sweatshirts and velour) of materials without any issue.  The bonus is that the magnet doesn't harm your material - no matter what it is.  The holder stays in place and is always ready.  I am a fan.  I did make an additional purchase of some extra magnets just in case I lose one - but the magnets are so strong you'd have to try pretty hard to lose them.

This is an awesome option for holding my readers. I have tried the neck leashes... they don't stay on the temples.. they are annoying as you are wearing your readers. This broach is the perfect answer. I waited until I had used it several times on various materials to write my review.
PROS
- The magnet is really strong. I have worn on thinner, thicker material without any issues. Some of the materials I have worn were nylon/dry weave, the picture I am sharing is a velour. I've worn with sweatshirts, flannel, cotton tshirts and even one of my favorite waffle weave shirts - with NO issues.
- The magnet attachment means no holes in your clothing.
- You can place the broach basically anywhere you want to place it on your shirt.
- The stones are sparkly and cute
- Reasonably priced
CONS
- So far, none - in my opinion.
I did purchase some spare disc magnets - just in case I misplace the back. That might be a good idea. But honestly, the magnet is so strong you'd have to try pretty hard to lose!

I purchased this to wear after a tub or shower - or when I wanted to comfortably lounge.  I am 5'7" - usually wear a 14/16 or 0x/1x - 38DDD - this robe hit me below the knee and is comfortable in fit.  If you like a super loose fit, I would recommend sizing up.  The zipper is full length and pockets on both sides.  Washed and dried without any issues.  It is a thinner cotton (meaning not fleece).

I purchased this to wear just out of the tub or shower... it's comfortable, easy to wear.  Washed and dried without issues.  I am 5'7" and hits me just below the knees - so it's perfect for me.  Bonus - POCKETS both sides!  Love that. I usually wear a 14/16 or 0x/1x - it fits nicely - not snug.  If you like a super loose fit, you'll want to size up.

This is a black wallet that appears to be alligator (?)... it looks like alligator skin - doesn't really feel like leather - the inside might be cow hide, but I am not sure.  The lining is crunchy and kinda cheapy... disappointed by that part.  It is dyed black.  It is a fold over type wallet.  There are several slots for credit cards or ID and comes with a light brown dust cover bag with drawstring closure.

My husband is forced to travel for our business and he was looking for a smaller backpack/sling bag to put his small items that need to be easily accessed during flights.  This is a great size for your cell phone, small tablet, small amount of medications, etc. It is black.  Has a combination lock option that is attached to the bag (Sort of like Away luggage - hooking up the zipper heads to the lock).  The lock also has a TSA access point on the lock.  He likes it a lot - but he has not yet used on a flight (coming in a few days).  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

My husband doesn't wear cowboy boots, but he does wear work boots.  We are always on the search for a good work boot sock.  Husband is a big guy- spends 7 days a week in a work boot.  These socks are pretty thin - but they stay up and are snug, without being uncomfortable.  They are a gray color - which is perfect - white socks never last long.  They appear to reinforced in the right places, toes and heels - across the top of the foot.  I wish they were a bit thicker, but I am hopeful these will last for 6 months or more.  I never hesitate to update my review should new info seem useful.

I was really expecting these socks (purchased for my big guy husband) to be a bit better quality as they are described as heavy duty compression.  They are not.  The first time he put them on, they snagged.  Eh.  They are not "compression" in any way - but they appear to sort of stay up - but if they were compression - they would definitely stay up.  They are cotton socks - but far from heavy duty work socks.  They will last for a few washes as far as I can see.  Disappointed.  We are using them right now as holders for ice packs for knees and elbows.  Not exactly what we'd hoped for using them for (on work feet!) but we have repurposed them.  I just cannot recommend them as heavy duty work/compression socks for men who really work.

They list the stone as 6.5 mm... it's about 1 1/2 carat in size - and in my opinion is realistic looking.  The spring ring clasp chain is just ok - I am not a fan of the spring ring clasp - but I had intended on removing it anyway.  I took the pendant off the chain and strung it on a solid white gold sparkle chain I own - worked perfectly.  Stays forward while on.  The stone has a realistic looking sparkle and I love traveling with pieces like this - hopefully will we all get to travel again this next year.  It is platinum plated which will help it not look tarnished - which I appreciate.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

This is much more beautiful in person.  The platinum plating will help it avoid tarnishing.  It has a lot of sparkle and in my opinion - it is believable.  The top is loose of main pendant and helps the pendant move and pick up light.  The chain is a surprise.  Very, very nice box chain - so it's strong and has it's own mirror sparkle - additionally it's super adjustable and has a lobster claw clasp with a heart weight at the adjust end - so it will stay in place.  In all honesty, this is just my favorite right now.  I love to travel with these pieces - so I am looking forward to a world of travel again soon!  Having said all of those things, it is plated.  I hope that it will last a long time - and I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

My husband is a 5x and the 5x fit him like a glove - not too snug - but cozy.  The battery unit charged up fairly quickly - however - they power is a bit short running - 2 1/2 hours on high.  There are 3 warming settings - high - medium and low.  The REAL bummer is that you apparently cannot wash this jacket!  For nearly two hundred dollars and you cannot wash it?  I am afraid that while it sounds really cool to have a warming jacket - as my husband was really looking forward to - it loses some of that luster when you realize that it will be dirty (100% cotton) and you will not be able to safely wash it.  Eh.  He should have more carefully read the ad page - there is not a single mention of cleaning - but he's a dude and cleaning wasn't the top of his list. In the end he decided to return it. Probably a good choice.

I was really bummed that the stones are soooo small they have next to zero sparkle.  They are just cz so why are they so small?  The setting is a bit rough - might snag clothing - but I've had rougher rings.  The sizing was fairly accurate.  Just wish that they would have enlarged the stones so there would be some sparkle - right now, there isn't even a tiny bit of sparkle.

Wow.  I am surprised that no one else has noted the sizing.. but I usually wear a 6 1/2 - 7.  I purchased the 8 because the ring is sort of wider... the ring I received SAID "size 8" but it measures just barely over a size SIX (6) on a ring wand!  I am really disappointed.  I ordered a size 9 to compare and then I will decide if I will return them both or not.  I am hoping this was just a mismarked/mistake.  The ring itself is really beautiful in person.  I really hope I can get one that fits.  At this price you would hope the sizing would be more accurate.

I purchased these as a gift for a friend who lives near my daughter - my daughter's wrist is shown in the photo.  Her wrist is small to average.  Daughter was doing the wrapping for me.  I am disappointed at the HUGE, chunky charm.  It really overwhelms the bracelet and I think looks a bit trashy.  I purchased more delicate, pretty bracelet for this person and my daughter is going to throw these in with it  - but against my vote.  In all honesty if I were there in person, I would not.  The charm is just so junky.  They are stretchy, pull on style.  They appear to possibly be actual stone, but it is hard to tell.  On the other hand, always remember that you usually get what you pay for and these are fairly inexpensive. I won't talk about the shipping delays because in this time of virus and shipping issues - but they were seriously missing for a long time before arriving just in time.

I purchased a red and a green one.  These are plus sizing... be sure to review the size chart before ordering.  The red is a christmas red and the green is a pine green.  The sleeves are good (one of my pet peeves is too short sleeves).  The neck is loose, but fitted.  Another pet peeve - too tight turtle necks.  They washed and dried nicely, I didn't notice any shrinkage. The body is nice and loose... definitely not form fitting - comfortable.

Ok.. I don't think this sweater is super ugly... I like it.  But it is SUPER scratchy.  I am hoping a good wash will help it.  The colors are bright and christmasy.  It is men sizing... I like a loose sweater so I went xx - the sleeves are long.. but the body sizing is not too tight over my chest (38DDD) I'll update my review if new info seems useful.

I purchased this to go with some pajamas that I got earlier to wear on Christmas morning - they have a lace trimmed neckline and it's the same color (slighty different lace but close enough) and matches the pajamas perfectly.  Machine washed and dried nicely.  Very lightweight... but comfortable and will be delightful for Christmas morning.

I purchased this set to wear with a robe and slippers for Christmas morning... it is a lovely rich burgundy color.  The lace is soft.  Machine washed and dried nicely.  I am 5'7"... 38DDD.. usually wear a 14/16 or 0x/1x - but I sized up just in case to the 18.  That was a good call.  But I don't like things tight across my chest.  The pants are a bit loose at the waist, but I don't like tight pajamas!

Purchased these to go with some pjs and robe of the same color for Christmas morning.  They have little rubber pieces on the bottom for no slipping.. the socks are a velour yarn knit and they are very thick.  Toasty.  I probably am not going to wear them everyday, but for christmas morning.. perfect.

I wanted some fun pieces for when we all video chat this holiday... and this fit the obvious bill.  There are lights.. 3 different settings... some flash and one solid on (which is the one I will be using as flashing lights are no good for most people).  The wires for the lights are black and wiry - so you need to sort of move them around a bit to hide, but after that... it's a great headband.  It comes loaded with a tabbed battery, remove the battery for use.  It will be great for one season.  2021 I am hoping we will all be TOGETHER after this awful virus.  Stay safe and well!

I was hoping the actual tunic would be as lovely as the photos and I was not disappointed.  It's a rich burgundy color and lots of gold thread, a few old gold sequins.  I am 5'7", 38DDD and usually wear a 14/16 or a 0x/1x.  I did size up to 2x because I am really more comfortable if it is looser over the bust area, and the 2x is perfect - for me - especially with the buttons - which are functional.  It is machine wash/dry... delicate.  And it did great!  It is a longer length, tunic like.  I am trying to find a darker burgundy legging to pair with it - but it works with jeans... or black leggings too.  I really like this tunic - perfect for the holiday and the all video chats to come.

This is a very simple, inexpensive watch.  It is my husbands GO TO watch.  He has a collection of very expensive watches.  But this is the watch he wears for work -  They last for about 3 or 4 years of heavy abuse.  He keeps buying and buying them.  Because they work - simple as that.  He particularly loves the ones with the bright orange band.  To each his own!

I purchased with Christmas in mind... the photo comes off burgundy and green - but in person it is very much a green and orange/red.  It still works but I would have preferred the burgundy!  Washed and dried beautifully (delicate wash and very light tumble).  It will be nice and cozy for winter days and nights. The material is soft and a little fuzzy.  I am 5'7"... 38DDD and usually wear a 14/16 or 0x/1x.  It long... draping below my knees in front because of the style and maybe just above the back of knee in the back.

I am 5'7".... 38DDD, usually wear a 14/16 or 0x/1x.  I sized up because this shirt is listed as "fitted" and prefer a looser fit - was a good idea.  The sleeves are nice and long... washed and dried nicely without obvious shrinkage -  I washed on delicate and tumbled on extra low for about 20 minutes and hung the rest.  It's soft and comfortable.  The neck is fitted but not super snug.  It's a rich burgundy - some call wine - it's not orangish.  I like it.

I purchased these for my husband and I to wear in video chats with family... trying to step up a little fun as we cannot gather this year...  These are a loose knit... very cute.  I think with careful use they will last for several seasons. They are a christmas red with soft white trim.  In my opinion one of the cutest versions of santa hats out there.

This tshirt is really thin.  It is a rich red and has long sleeves.  I sized up as I prefer a looser fit, I would definitely recommend sizing up.  It is longer (tunic like) and I like that.  It does wrinkle terribly.  I haven't washed/dried it just yet but I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.  I would just say this blouse isn't nearly as "hearty" in person (the photo looks thicker - heartier to me) - very thin, but soft material.

I was really disappointed at how thin this blouse was - wrinkled.. looks really cheap.  I am going to wash and hang dry it - but I think this blouse is headed for the trash bin.  The color is rich and burgundy -ish.  The neckline is small V. I sized up because I did see the rayon content and I like a looser fit - I would definitely recommend sizing up - if you really  want it.  I had high hopes for this blouse, but eh.

I am on the fence about this shirt... I like a looser fit so I sized up - that was good call.  It is a nylon material - that is sort of soft - but not really cozy.  The colors are soft and the "tie dye"  is well done... the colors are bit odd - I can't explain beyond that, but I don't rate for my personal opinion of colors.  I think their photos are not really showing the coloring accurately.  Always a challenge.  The sleeves are long.. I consider that a positive. I machine washed and then tumbled on low - seems ok.  I don't think it's gonna be my fav - but I will wear it while it's cooler. I kinda wish there'd been a darker color palate to choose from - jewel tones?

Ok.. this is my first purchase of the eye glass leashes for myself... so there is apparently a learning curve.  the corded leashes are a little better but maybe not as "cool" as the beaded ones... But I have had one of the beaded leashes on for less than an hour and already I've had to replace the silicone loop on one side and there are wires sticking out of the beaded leash portion that are pokey.  Eh.  Now, on the plus side, they send a little bag of spare parts - good thing - but nevertheless, the beaded, more stylish leashes may just go in the trash and I'll use the the cords - no pokey wires.  The other thing... they could be about 6 inches longer...  but then, I'm learning.  Maybe there is a good reason they are so short.  All in all, I would skip the cool beaded leashes and stick to the more boring cords - in my opinion.  OR... this could also be a case of "you get what you pay for" thing.  Perhaps I should try some more expensive beaded leashes.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

I believed I was purchased a women's shirt, this is clearly sized for a man, so while I sized up (because of the cotton content) - it is HUGE.  Very disappointed.  The sleeves are 4 to 6" too long.  The body is like a tunic on me and I am not a small person - 5'7" - 38DDD.  Just swims on me.  The snowflake is print is really sticky...  Overall, I would not purchased this again.

UPDATE BELOW...........After reading other reviewers reports and reviewing the sizing chart, I purchased the xl. I usually wear a 14/16 or 0x/1x. I am 5'7". They fit comfortably snug. If you're a loosey goosey undie wearer, I would size up. I went out on a limb and ordered the black, the gray and the beige (or nude - it is a very yellowish beige). It was risky for sure. They are mostly cotton with a little spandex for comfort. They washed and dried nicely. They are interestingly sewn, where some thought was truly given in construction. There is a 1/2" elastic waist - which is zig-zag sewn to keep it in place. The crotch is a wide, but possibly a little short. They are high waisted, over my belly button and fit snugly - maybe a slight bit of flattening, but they are not meant to be girdle undies. The older I've gotten the more I am into comfort and the so called "granny panties." Whatever works. The black is a deep, true black - the gray is heathered and the beige/nude is a little yellowy or buttery to me - just a little. Overall, I like these undies. I included some pics of the gray pair because it seemed to be easy to see the stitching, etc... Having said all that, I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful!
UPDATE: 11/30/2020.... Ok.. after wearing these for a while I am back to purchase more. I wear the gray the most so that is what I buy. I am not very fond of the neutral/nude/beige color.... it's yellowish to me. So I am sticking with mostly gray and black - but that is personal choice. They have washed and dried beautifully multiple times. Snug, but comfortable. Holds your tummy in just a little. Stay up. Honestly, these are some of the best cotton undies I've purchased in a while!
UPDATE: 12/4/2020... purchased a few more. These are really good so far. Hope they last as well as they fit!

I purchased this shirt to go with some holiday leggings I just got... I am 5'7"... 38DDD/E - I sized up when I saw the cotton content as I like a looser tunic top - that seems to have been a good idea.  I have not washed yet, but I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.  The colors are soft - not overly bright.  The t is thinner material and soft.  I love it.

I purchased the red plaid and black/white snow flakes... my adult daughter (who lives in Arizona) chose the red plaid and the turquoise reindeer colors.  They are a thinner tshirt material.  Very comfortable.  Very holiday!  I purchased some tunic-like tops to wear with mine - we are both choosing to wear them inside for lounging and not "out."  They'd be great pajamas because they are so soft and comfy.  I would also say that the sizing was spot on.  So get your waist measurements and use their chart.  They are pretty thin material, so I'm hoping they don't pill and wear too thin - time will tell and I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

Ok.  I've left my cell phone in the cart TWICE.  I'm done doing that.  So I purchased this purse in hopes of avoiding that whole scenario again.  It is faux leather.  Comes with 2 handles... one crossover body style (I'm using that one) and the other is a really short one I can't imagine ever using.  The handles have swivel hook attachments.  There is a pocket for your phone (the clear side) so you can technically touch and use your phone (I have a samsung S10+ and fit and worked.  I don't really think I will be "using" the clear side much... but it WILL keep my phone ON me.  *sigh*  There is also another slot that you could slip a few dollars, your drivers license and maybe a credit card.  In these days of virus I have taken to sticking my drivers license and a credit card in my bra rather than having to carry a purse that you have set down or on something.  I just think, IN THEORY, this purse should really help with a lot of things.  Time will tell and I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful!

I purchased this hat for a friend going thru chemo.  With winter arriving, I thought this looked warm and cute.  She loved it.  I purchased one for myself after seeing the one I purchased for her.  The brim is lightly wired so it stays out - where it should be - adds to the style.  Felty material.  Cute flowers on the side. Black accented with gray - just as shown.

I purchased 2 different types and these are my favs of the two.  they come up way higher on the top of my feet, which works for the shoes I wear and the fit.  I have a size 7 foot and an 8 1/2 (womens) foot due to a deformity.  There are 3 little zig zag silicone strips that help to keep the sock in place on your heel.  I purchased the black and they are a dark black.  The sock is fairly thin, but not see through.  The socks are very stretchy.  I find them comfortable.  I probably will buy more of these.  Oh, and as for washing, I have a delicate bag and I wash them in the bag and then lay them flat to dry in my dryer closet.  Having said all of that, I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

These work well... but I have to report that if you have a size 7 1/2 or smaller foot, these will easily slip off.  Due to having had polio when I was a child, I have one foot that is a size 7 and the other that is an 8 1/2.  So I am uniquely able to discuss this discovery.  They fit perfectly on my 8 1/2 foot... but with my smaller foot, I just can't keep the sock on.  There is a silicone heel back - which will help if the sock fits your foot.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

Ok..I made a mistake. This bra is NOT thinly foam lined. It's just 2 pieces of nylon. I wear a 38DDD. This is a front loader - so there are not back hook adjustments. It cuts pretty hard - so I would NOT consider this bra "all day comfort" as the tag says. On the upside, the underwire is lifting. But, the cup was not enough for my bust. I went back and ordered the 40DDD to see if that helps with cup and comfort. The do offer a 38G - but I worry about the tightness. Listen, you do want a pretty snug fit - because the elastic will stretch. And in this case there are no tighter hooks to go to. But it was so uncomfortable I didn't think I could take the wait to a stretched out band. The straps are about 3/4" wide and are adjustable. I do wish there was a thin foam lining. Wacoal is a good brand and it appears well made. There is a slight decor of stretch lace at the cleavage V - so it gives a bit of feminine edge. The lace lays flat and I didn't notice it under my T shirt. The back strap is NOT terrible wide. So if you're afraid of "back fat" this might not be the bra for you. It hit ok under the arm for me but I can see that some may have issues with fat there too. All in all it's not my least favorite but I can't wear my size. U really like a front loader, but it is SUPER hard to find one built for my bust size I guess. Even harder doing it all online. I will check in after I get the 38G to compare the fit and update my review with that info in case if might be helpful.
UPDATE... The 38G rib cage fit was a slightly more comfortable feel and the cups were deeper... but... the way the underwire is sewn it pushes down on your cleavage creating a very big cleavage bulge.. Eh. While I really tried to make this style work, it doesn't for me. I am returning both. I would say that if you are DDD or larger, I would pass on this style. Having said that.. Wacoal is a very good brand and well made. I would try some other styles. I plan to.

I purchased the 38DD - REALLY needed the DDD or E/F (which is not offered). There really isn't any "separation" and I did miss the part where it didn't have an underwire.
PROS: I like the neutral color. The straps were wide at the right spots - IF you criss/cross them. A nice wide back strap, four hooks. It looks very well made.
CONS: The material snags really easily. If you don't criss cross the straps - the wide part of the strap is too far down your chest - thus cutting into your shoulder (I get that I don't have small boobs - but this hurts after a while). No underwire for better lift. While it looks well made, it is VERY --- not feminine. Looks like a battle bra - which I was willing to go with if it would have just given me the right fit.
Most of my stars are construction. One star for snaggy material and straps that aren't wide where they need to be. This LOOKS like a bra meant for women with larger busts - but honestly, it's not really made very well for that - in my opinion.

I purchased these to wear over the top of my plastic "spa" bags I put on my feet.  It never fails I need to run around - lotions and plastic bags  while walking around is a recipe for disaster.  Now.. you shouldn't walk around a lot but I have found these socks really help keep the bag in place, minimize the slide around inside the bag and are washable.  I set them out to dry after washing - otherwise the rubber bottoms stick to everything else in the dryer.  Mainly gray with black  - the black is mainly the top of the foot - and the rubber pieces on the bottoms.  Oh.  And I wear a women's regular size 8 1/2".  They fit perfectly - not too tight at the ankle and not loose. If you have a long foot, they might not be as comfortable.

I purchased 2 of these.. one for me... one for my friend/neighbor who is going through chemo.  With winter coming I thought she might like this.  It's warm, but lightweight.  The flowers are really a pretty touch.  I pull my down to my ears - but the hat has an edge that you wear sort of beret?  I am happy with it and I think my friend will love hers.

My friend, who is going through chemo, has chosen baseball caps as her hat choice right now... this one is very cute.  The photo kinda makes the hat look a little purpley... but the one I received is predominately pink.  They are all probably a little different. She will like it.

I purchased this hate for a friend going through chemo.  I thought it was really cute.  The brim is actually wired.  So it stays out.  Really cute.  I hope she likes it and it gives her a little spring in her step. God bless her.

I purchased this for a friend who is going through chemo and lost her hair.  She has a wonderful strength and sense of humor so I thought she'd enjoy this hat.  I will give it to her soon.  But I have to say, I LOVE this cap - in fact, I'm thinking about ordering one for myself.  It's simply glorious.  The inside is a poly knit - The rabbit fur outer is really beautiful and lively.  It's soft and beautiful.  I was so pleasantly surprised.  I think my friend is going to love it as much as I do.

I purchased these for a dear friend who has lost her hair due to chemo.  They are thinner than I thought.. but she reports they are comfortable.  I purchased the wine and dark gray set.  I think she likes them. Hopefully we can just throw them out when her hair comes back.  Period.

Tshirt material is nice and soft... but I would size up (I actually sized up 2 sizes to the women's 3x because I like a looser fit across the chest - 38DDD) Washed and dried nicely - no noticeable shrinkage.  The print patch is not too stiff.
I purchased these for my husband - who has a bit of a wide foot.  I did size up for that and it seems a good choice.  He reports they are comfortable.  He uses them for house slippers.
Really nice quality, long sleeve Biden/Harris 2020 tshirt.  I would say size up if you like a looser fit.  Washed and dried well, not much shrinkage, if any.

I wanted to replace some regular laces with stretch laces for convenience.  They are a bit of trick to do, but there are directions are just ok.  The last piece that you have to press together to "lock" into place takes quite a bit of finger strength.  I ended up using some wide pliers I had to achieve the lock.  They are adjusted by the pinch and slide piece at the top of the shoe.. They are ok. I'm probably not going to this again - but I will say these were WAY better than the corkscrew stretch laces - those looked terrible.  These look far better.

Eh.  Was thinking it would be nice to just be able to slip on my shoes and not have to tie the laces.  These were awful. They twisted and looked really awkward in the shoe - and weren't long enough to be actually helpful.  I pulled them back out and they would not properly recurl.  I actually tossed them. Lesson learned.

Purchased for my grandson who live in Arizona.  I have 2 grandsons who are 6 and 9.  Mom reports they fit well and I would say the sizing is accurate - meaning you don't need to size up or down.  Quick and easy with velcro (like) closing.

This is a men's t... so it hard to fit properly over my bust area. Because its 100% cotton I went up 2 sizes. Good thing (im a 38DDD) because while I didn't notice a lot if shrinkage - but I dont like tight across the chest. Honestly I love the sentiment of the shirt- but I need to find a women's version.

This shirt is 100% cotton, so I am really glad I sized up to the 2x - I don't like tight tshirts across my chest (38DDD).  Turned out to be a good choice, however, after washing and drying, I didn't notice any shrinkage.  Even though the t shirt lists as 100% cotton, it is not stiff at all - very soft and fairly lightweight.  It will be around longer than I need it to be.

I purchased a number of different T's and this one of my favs.  The material is very soft, comfortable and lightweight - but not see through.  I usually wear a xl or 1x.  I chose to size up the 2x after reading the measurements and because I do not like a tight tshirt over my bust (38DDD).  Plus, the material is 50/50 - it's possible there could be some shrinkage, but I didn't notice any.  I machine washed in cold - dried on extra low - no problem.  I really like the t's and happy with my purchase - they arrived really quickly too (I do have prime - but in this day and age of the virus - that sometimes doesn't really matter).

I purchased a number of different T's and this one of my favs.  The material is very soft, comfortable and lightweight - but not see through.  I usually wear a xl or 1x.  I chose to size up the 2x after reading the measurements and because I do not like a tight tshirt over my bust (38DDD).  Plus, the material is 50/50 - it's possible there could be some shrinkage, but I didn't notice any.  I machine washed in cold - dried on extra low - no problem.  I really like the t's and happy with my purchase - they arrived really quickly too (I do have prime - but in this day and age of the virus - that sometimes doesn't really matter).
Purchased these for my grandson (9 years) in another state.  Mom reports they fit as expected and he loves them.
I purchased these for my grandsons after making the mistake of purchasing some other non-name brand shoes.  Mom reports they fit as expected and the boys like them.

Purchased these for my 6 year old and 9 year old grandsons... they appear to accurate in sizing and look really well sewn.  Washed and dried well.  Material dries rapidly when worn and wet.  The legs are a bit longer - which is the style currently.  The boys like them - but they aren't super particular regarding clothing.

For a name brand like Columbia - I expected a better quality.  The sizing runs a little small and the zipper is very temperamental.  I probably wouldn't purchase again due to the zipper failure rate.  I end up having to do the zipper for my grandson because it is so troublesome more often than not.  The inner lining zips up into the zipper and the zipper head often doesn't pull up properly - won't grab the start teeth.  Eh.

Ok.. If you're buying the box because it's filled with pirate booty - Eh.  They are really, really cheap plastic items that are truly just garbage.  There is a paper map - on thicker paper - might be the only thing that is of some quality - but not really useful in any way.  The only thing I left in the boxes (I purchased 2) was a plastic skull and cross bone ring.  There are some really thin plastic coins that are truly ugly - threw those in the trash too.  I separately purchased some metal gold coins, glass jewels, faux pearl necklaces and some other trinkets.  I then buried them in my sandy front yard.  The box is a fairly good size.. and will work- has base metal pieces on the outside. The slide hook closure sometimes closes, sometimes you have to really wrank on it to make it work.  Eh.  As any empty box and 1/2 the price - maybe?  All of these things together, I would not purchase again.
These are a great, worn color.  Have the adjustable button waist which is what my grandsons need.  Skinny legs, but NOT tight.  I like them. True to size in my opinion.
I have thinner, smaller grandsons and the adjustable button waist is just a necessity.  These are great.  Washed and dried beautifully and fit perfectly.
I purchased these for my grandson because it had the adjustable waist buttons - they work great.  The denim is stretchy and he likes these.

These fit my 6 and 9 year grandsons perfectly.  They come with croakies - which they don't care for - and a nice drawstring case.  They wrap slightly around their faces which I think is a good thing.  Blue and yellow plastic and blackish lens.  They are polarized.  They both seem to be very comfortable wearing them and I don't notice them slipping or falling off either.  All good.

Daughter reports these fit perfectly - my grandson is a smaller, thinner guy - they are meant to fit snugly but not too tight and they do.  Daughter also reported that they washed and dried nicely without any visible shrinkage.

My grandsons LOVE this dial shoe the most.  I was afraid I wasn't going to be able to get them again for a while - was very happy to see they brought them back.  A bit of a trick to learn about how to tighten and loosen the "shoe strings" but once you do it - it's totally a breeze.  They love the look and colors.  Purchased one pair for my 6 year old grandsons and another for my  9 year old grandson.  The 9 year old is the biggest fan.  I would say they are true to size.

My daughter reports that these fit my 10 year grandson well - he is a smaller, thin guy.  They are meant to fit snugly but not too tightly and that is apparently just how they fit.  Daughter reports they washed and dried nicely without noticeable shrinkage.

The shoes ran true to size in my opinion.  HOWEVER... the ad is SO deceptive.  They show the last three letters of the "brand"  as IKE.... Now.  What do you THINK you're buying?  A brand name we all know - NIKE.  BUT YOU ARE NOT getting a nike brand boys shoe.  You're getting a chinese knockoff... something like Vicike... or something - who cares what it says.  I was furious.  I purchased two pairs of the black shoes thinking I was getting something I was not.  They are cheap foreign junk - that isn't cheap.  GRRRRRR.  I am searching for a brand I know and trust and hopefully not fully made in China.  As always - BUYER BEWARE. However when the seller is posting extremely misleading photos - shame on them.  I would pass on these - across the board.
These are ok.  They are pretty soft - not too stiff.  The waist is good for boys that are hard to fit. They are pull on.  The sizing seems good.

I purchased these for my grandsons - two different sets - one is 10 the other is 7.  They are great.  Not too thin, not too thick.  Washed and dried nicely.  I like them.  I think they are pretty accurate in sizing too.
These are super cute train pajamas.  They are a snug fit - so you might want to size up 1 size.  They washed and dried nicely.
These are really cute swim trunk/board shorts.  They are long... and washed and dried nicely.  They are soft and I think my grandsons are gonna love them.  Just looking at them I would not size up.
These are ok... they are soft - not super stiff.  Washed and dried nicely.  Cotton.  I would size up.  I wish I would have.  But they'll be ok for this season.  Very basic.

In my opinion these are pretty thick and stiff.  I did wash and dry them - I don't think they shrank much - but - eh.  The color is not as good in person either.. it's a weird sort of mustardy khaki?  It's hard to describe.

These are great.  Wash and dried nicely - did shrink a little.  If I were to order again - I would size up one size or two if you like a looser fit.  These are meant to be snug fitting.  The prints are adorable and just as shown in the pics.
These run really large - in my opinion I was size down 1 size.  I mean, you don't want them sloppy large and these are even though I purchased my grandsons' actual sizes.

These are great.  Cotton, soft and washed and dried nicely.  My ten year old grandson likes them.  In my opinion the may run a tiny large... so I would not size up.  They did shrink a tad, but not enough to size up.
These are great.  Soft, washed and dried nicely.  Fit well.  My ten year grandson loves them. And you get a nice number of them too.
This is a nice, thin rain jacket.  Good quality.  I would be careful with sizing... maybe runs a little small.  If I ordered again, I would go up one size.
I was little disappointed in the sizing... seems to run pretty small.  I would size up 2 sizes as in addition it shrinks some.
These are great - thin, cotton.  A little stretch and it did shrink just a tad.  But I planned for that and sized up one size. Perfect.

The the shirts are really big - the sizing seems off.  So I purchased the size I thought would work and it's huge.  The sleeves are ridiculous too long.  We try to roll them up but they don't stay up.  I know you want the sleeves to be long - but I will have to them altered.

I am 5'7" .. usually wear a 38DDD/F (I ordered a 40DD in this bra after reading some other reviews)...I chose the nude color.  It is a fairly good fit.  I hooked comfortably on the second from the last hook (perfect is hooking to the last - because the elastic stretches as you wear it - it loosens).. the cup is VERY lightly lined - which I like - no nips... the straps are adjustable and wider - a little more than 1/2".. But they do still cut into my shoulders (partly because boobs are heavy - eh) ..NO wire - which I know is appealing, BUT it isn't as lifting as a wire - so there's that.  The back strap has 4 hooks and is very wide.  NO buldging under the arm and no fat rolls in the back area - but I think part of that is going up one rib cage size.  The bra is soft and fairly comfortable...The cup coverage is full - but I still had significant cleavage - another challenge of full boobs.  It does have a little lace/pretty at the V of the cleavage - but it is minimal and doesn't show under a Tshirt I tried on with it. So over all it is better than average - but not perfect in my opinion. But lets face it bras are SUPER personal and if you got girls, this one is worth a shot. I have many pairs of skechers and because of a foot deformity I have to buy 2 different sizes - I say this because  a good fit on a pair of pull on shoes is critical.  These are an interesting version - the tops are higher up your foot than others.  But the top is stretchy. The sole is a different print than some - less spread out and deep - which is good because my favorite pair of skechers has that wide, deep sole print and it picks up all the little rocks from everywhere.  I purchased the black tops with black soles.  I personally don't like a white sole unless the tops are white or light colored. UPDATE BELOW.....Wow. These suitcases are AWESOME for the price! I was expecting a lot less. I purchased 3. I chose the bright, safety orange. Easy to see in the sea of other colors for sure. The outer shell is hard - sturdy feeling. The inside is roomy. They are around 5-6" deep in each side (I've included some pics - sorry about holding the ruler upside down - but you can see it's about 5 to 6") One side is zipper closed, the other side has elastic straps to contain contents. The outer zippers are sturdy feeling and run smoothly. There is also an 'extender' zipper giving you an extra 2" or so in depth. Honestly, these suitcases 'feel' as nice as some others I have purchased for literally 4x the price! All the nice care points too with the inner zipper, and additional pockets. It looks and feels like a suitcase way beyond the price point. Now, in all fairness, they are new to me and I will be using them on a trip in about a week. So, I will update my review should new info seem useful. The ad says they weigh just under 10lbs.. I used my handheld suitcase scale and it registered 9.1 lbs empty. One of the pics I included shows me holding a 12" ruler so you can get an idea of the width of the inside of the cases. The pull out handle is not as 'sturdy' feeling as I would like... but it works- It locks down and lifts easily. It appears or feels like the pullout handle is plastic at the handle part.. the bar appears to be lightweight aluminum - so it might be bent with lots of use.. we'll see. There are two grab handles one on the top and one the side of case. The outer wheels are multi-directional... and stick out quite a bit and I'm not sure they are as 'sturdy' as the rest of the case. If there is an 'Achilles heel' on these bags... the wheels may be it or possibly the pull handle.. but we as I said, time will tell and I never hesitate to update. I've included some pics and hope my review was helpful to you.
UPDATE: 12/4/2019.... Have taken these suitcases through the airline system two times so far... NO problems to report. I am happier and happier with this purchase... but time will tell and I update if new info seem useful.

I am 5'7", 0x or 14/16 38DDD... and the dress fits as I expects... maybe a bit smaller than you expect.  The colors are bright white and navy.  It does have pockets and is a "mock type wrap."  Meaning... that the "wrap" is just over the chest... and there is no tie in any place.  It creates a bit of a "v" neck. I will be tacking the V a little higher so I don't have so much cleavage because that's what I like.  The sleeves are short, as shown in the photo on the ad page.  The dress hits at the top of my feet, so it's long.  I love navy and white... the flower print is lovely.  I purchased this because I travel and I love to bring things that don't wrinkle and are easy to wash and wear.  I washed on my machine's hand wash cycle and then hung it to dry.  I am going to pop in the tumble dryer on low and see how it does.  I think it will be fine. Now... the down side... anywhere the material "stretches"... it thins... and becomes see through.  Ugh.  Fortunately I don't see any sensitive parts where that is an issue. The issue for me is two spots under each arm because my bosom pushes the front out and the area under my arm is where you see the stretch.  When I saw this, I stretched the main part of the skirt and was unhappy to see it thins right out and becomes mostly see through...  So... I won't be wearing this dress for anything more than a lounging in the privacy of my hotel room and possibly be ok to answer the door for room service... but I probably would not wear this dress out on a sunny day... or night with lights showing through me?  :)  Everyone has a different comfort level.  I just want you to be aware.  It's ALMOST wrinkle free.. but not the best I've seen or have.  But for the price point... for me, it's going to work for private lounging. I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.  I hope my review has been helpful to you!

I am pleasantly surprised by the quality of this purse - especially for the price.  It comes with a tag that says it is genuine leather.. however, I am not a leather expert... it LOOKS like its leather... The bag construction is very stout.  It's thick.. stiff/sturdy - but not too heavy.  The straps are about 1" wide.  I did provide some photos so you can get a better look.  That little bump on the front is a croc head... very cute.  The flap, which has a magnetic clasp that works well and without much effort, hides a zippered closure.  Great for security and to keep things in your purse, in your purse.  The bottom is slightly rounded and even when loaded it doesn't really stand on it's own without being leaned up on something.. so that inner zipper is great.  The interior is lined with a brown satin like material - see photo.  Inside you'll find a large, wide zippered compartment and an wide slide in slot.  On the outer back of the bag there is another zippered compartment.  The straps configure to use as a backpack OR just pull up on the strap and VIOLE! it's a shoulder strap that you can actually do cross body on if you want - and I am NOT a tiny person.  The top of the strap is slightly padded - a nice thought in useful design.  There is also, as you can see in the ad photo, a sewn grab handle at the top - great for grabbing and going.  I was also pleasantly surprised at how much I could get into it.  I forgot to add a pic showing the width... it's about 3" but with a fold spread that makes it about 4 1/2".  The outer part of the bag has a new leather "sheen" to it... a little shiny... but if it is really fine leather, it will dull with use and age and take on a nice worn patina.  We'll see about that.  Honestly, if this bag had a designer sponsor, it would be a $500-$1500 bag. I have some LV - so that would be the top in number... :)  Of course it would then have their logo all over it. I should also mention it comes with a white, thin woven type dust cover bag.  I really love this bag right now. This is no junk bag. It's rich looking quality... fine, thoughtful details and price make it a trifecta of bags.  That is WIN WIN WIN. It arrived quickly (I have prime) and in excellent condition.  Having said all of that... I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seems useful.  I hope this info and the photos were helpful to you in shopping!

I am a total wig virgin... NEVER even tried on a wig... I just like this cut .. I grew out my bangs - it took about 6 months.. but every once in a while... I feel like bangs... this is a really cute option... obviously I am no cutie - just an old grandma... but honestly... this is fun. So much fun my sister bought one too. Hahaha! I can't get over how real it looks. I took it out of it's hair net... shook it once... ran my fingers through it once I put it on and Voile! It's VERY easy to put on... lots of adjustment - I must have a smaller head as I have it set on the tightest setting and it's still kinda loose.. (?) not sure about that... Even though it is synthetic... I still think it's passable.. even outside. I've included a few pics for you to judge yourself. Please be kind... I didn't blur out my face this time as I think its harder to evaluate the wig - :) I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful... and I definitely will update with how it goes to wear it over time... I'm new to all this. I did buy a gentle shampoo.. a special de-static styling brush and a "head" to store it on... Also... to be fair... a few of the wisps around my face are actually mine... I have blonde hair naturally, and it looks like more highlights - I just love the overall "wispyness" of the shag like cut. It's adorable. But then I am old and a child of the 70s. I tried provide you shots of all round and the top... honestly, it looks real to me. Like a really good hair hair cut... even in the sun! Best to all of you!

LeSportsac items are beautiful.. feather weight - and yet super tough.  This backpack is no exception.  The bag is fairly small compared to other "styles" of LeSportsac.. perfect for my use.  The straps are nylon - no padded, however they are very comfortable.  The top is zippered and there is a zipped pouch on the outside.  The best part for me is that the inside lining is a lovely light beige.  This makes it so much easier to find things at the bottom.  The stitched/quilted design on the outside of the bag is beautiful. The photographs shown are accurate.  There are two handles on the top of the bag so you can easily grab the backpack like a purse if that is more convenient at the moment.  One note:  You will want to keep the top zippered closed. Guess that happens with age and experience! :)  LeSportsac has me hooked.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.  Hope this info was useful to you!

The color is a sort of a metallic gold tan/beige. Very light in color.  The straps are a very light gray. The material does feel waterproof. More flexible than others I have tried. Ok.  This is tough... because I love the front of the purse/backpack... but HATE the woven cotton straps!!  If they were real leather to match the loveliness of the bag, it would be a home run.  So now... I have to find a leather worker to have appropriate leather straps made.  I just hate the cheap look and feel of the black cotton straps.  Beyond that... on the "up" side for me.... the interior is a lovely turquoise - nylon like material.  Win/Win.  Wipeable and easy to find things in the bottom.  If it's dark interior it's harder - or at least for me.  There is a zipper top under the stylish flap.  Good for security.  There are two room pockets inside and on the other side a zippered patch.  The outside, under the flap there is another zippered pocket... it is lined with a dark gray cotton/blend - or at least that's how it feels. I love the brushed metal studs that line the outside flap with a loop and leather strip for flare.  I just don't understand why they stopped with all the fine leather and made really awful cotton straps. .. Also it sort of "converts to a cross-over by pulled the back pack straps up, making a cross body length loop.  Eh.  Not really my fav.  And they sewed a cotton hand strap to the back/top - for a hand grab... that's "ok" and more useful than the crossbody conversion.. However,  I am going to see if there is a way to replace that the hand grab with leather too.  All in all... for the price of the bag... it should have had fine leather, wide straps.  Just disappointed that in order to really love the bag I need to invest more money and time to get it to look and function better.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

I purchased several different "brands" of these types of bras and you should know, they are all EXACTLY the same - so if you like this type of bra, go for the cheapest.  They literally are the same.  I am a 38DDD.  I really need and usually wear a severe underwire.  I was looking for a soft, wireless, weekend like bra - These offer little to NO support... but the straps are really comfortable and of course, NO underwire.  Just to be fair, they are in NO WAY  a "sports bra"  Unless yoga is the only thing you'll be doing.  They offer NO support for jossling the girls about.  I do leave the pads (which are removable) in  - wash in a lingerie bag and then dry flat in my dryer closet.  The pads are a bit of a pain to take in and out - so I don't.  But they do offer "nip" coverage if that matters to you and tiny bit of shape help. These are really comfy - but are really just sleep or weekend at home bras for me. Definitely chose the least expensive offer - they truly are exactly all the same.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

I personally leave them in - wash on delicate, in a lingerie bag and the dry them flat in my dryer closet.  So I don't have an issue with the pads. I was hoping a little more.  The bottom does roll up as you're wearing them.. BUT the wide straps are super comfortable. These are are in NO WAY "sports" bra - but possibly a yoga bra -  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

UPDATE BELOW 6/26/2018....
If you HAVE been using the Vanity Fair front closing - 75339, BUT you have been WISHING for more coverage - THIS version is slightly better. I've been wearing the other version for many, many years. I keep asking for and WISHING for a 38DDD - but to no avail so I buy the 38DD. Why? Because I find it comfortable and NOT over padded. I like a thin lining for comfort, but don't desire additional "size" with the filling. This version gives you ALMOST 1 more inch in the cup at the small size... at least on the 38DD. If you wore the 38DD in the "lighter" version, I would recommend buying the same size of this "fuller" version. I still really need a 38DDD, but I can definitely make this work. This version IS convertible to a racer back, just like the older version - although I rarely use that option, it's nice to have. Other improvements include - slightly wider - different material straps. FAR less "cutting" into the top of your shoulder - further - the back strap is slightly wider.. The front closure is still plastic - but I have yet to have one "fail." The down side of front closure bra is that as you wear it, as with ALL bras, the elastic "gives" and with the front closure, there are no adjustments. Still I get a fair amount of wear out each one - more than a year. For the price, I think this good. Camel Camel these so that you can grab them when they are an even better price - which has always beat what they are offered to me locally. There is a thin fiber fill - but it doesn't add bulk - just modesty. And the cup is smooth - there is some strategically placed lace that doesn't create lumps and bumps on the bra - a pretty touch. The other think I notice is that this newer version "feels" more supportive. I think there is a visible lift - meaning more support. The straps are adjustable, and as I said above, much more comfortable that the older, smaller version. Bras are SUPER personal and every one of us is different, but I was hoping to share enough details and comparisons to be helpful. I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seems useful. Honestly, I think this bra is worthy of shot if you liked the older version but were wishing for a bit more cup.
UPDATE 6/26/2018..... I just wanted to say that I ordered the 40DD. As I have mentioned above, I'd prefer a 38DDD or even an E... but that isn't offered in this style... so I decided to "try" the 40DD. I had feared it would be too big around, but it is actually fine... SLIGHTLY larger, but not significantly around... the "UP" part is that you get at LEAST 1/2" more in cup coverage. To me, that's worth while. Every woman is built uniquely and bras are SO hard, especially mail order. If you prefer a front loader... are full size.. and prefer an underwire for support, these are worth a try. I recommend possibly trying one size around "up" if you'd like a bit more cup.
I have had no issues with poor performance in wear or otherwise, however, I DO wash in a lingerie bags, on delicate and hang them to dry in my dryer "closet." Hope this helps you in some way!

Sadly, this jacket was ruined by the terribly sewn inner lining, especially around the tag. It's so awful It would be hard to sell at an super cut outlet. Further the seller appears to have known this when they sent it out stating that it didn't seem to effect the wear. Seriously? Not for this price. I have included pics for you to see. I did return the jacket immediately. I do feel compelled however to share my thoughts about the jacket
1. The fur appears to be mink. It is not very soft.. but that is because the mink has not been sheared, so the stiffer, longer hairs are still present. I own several very high quality minks and I usually choose the sheared pelts as they are so much softer. The color is good. Fairly deep and rich looking. The pic I included was with full sun on it to show the pelts and the knit better.
2. This jacket is "knitted" using remnants of mink pelts. The "knitting" means there is a slight "give" or stretch to the jacket. The sizing to me appears to be spot on. I did go one size larger in order to accommodate sweaters underneath. I had considered traveling with this jacket.
3. The inner lining is a very, very cheap crepe. After consulting with my furrier*, the quality of the crepe was so inferior in order to "repair" the lining sewing it would necessary to completely remove the lining and start over. Far more cost than the value of the jacket. He also advised me that the low quality of the lining material is such that it will tear and not bear up to regular wear. *Furrier is my storage, care and alteration specialist - NOT a fur salesmen.
4. The sleeves are extraordinarily long. I actually liked this, however, you should know you cannot fold the sleeve over as to the lining and sewing construction of the jacket. So the sleeves fell almost down to my finger tips. You can kind of see this on the model wearing the jacket.
5. The "crush" collar does in fact create a floppy hood of sorts, but is sticks out in odd angles because of the "shoulder" humps that keep the collar down around your shoulders when it is not a hood.
6. The jacket has fur hooks for closures down the front and at the neck if you decide to pull the hood up and use as a hood.
7. It claims to be, and SORT of is, a semi swing coat... meaning wider at the bottom than at the chest... sort of A shaped... HOWEVER.. the hem is so horribly sewn that it is terribly uneven all around. In fact, the hem looks as though it is longer at both hips and then shorter in the front and back. One side being longer than other. Further, it was quite long. Falling well below my knees and I'm 5'7".
Over all, it was just horrible construction. Dealing with this seller has been challenging as well. I will not be a return customer. Amazon had to step in to help facilitate the return. Shouldn't have been necessary as it was a straight up, immediate return.

UPDATED REVIEW BELOW
The jewelry box = about 4 stars
The musty, moldy STINK that permeates this entire box inside and out.... negative 5 stars... So 2 stars is very generous.
Honestly, I REALLY wanted to love this box. It has nice, handy drawers... they are softly lined and the dividers can be removed if you would like to use the drawers differently. It looks nice. The side flaps have nice pockets to hold longer necklaces in as you close the flaps. The mirror works if you are putting the box on top of a taller dresser... I was going to set it on my really big bathroom counter, so the mirror was really not useful for me. The top mirror flap is opened by lifting and then sort of held open with a thin ribbon. It does NOT lock - in case that matters you. There is a handle for lifting and moving the box if you desire - make sure the side flaps are snapped shut before moving. I noticed that having the side flaps open helped the top lid stay open.
Here is the bottom line... when I opened the box an OVERWHELMING musty, moldy odor nearly knocked me over. I don't consider myself to be overly sensitive to odors, however, this odor lingered in my living room for days. Now, this is supposed to be "leather." It does have a very, very faint smell of leather... but it is easily overtaken by the must and mold. :( I am attempting several strategies to remove the smell... but if you put anything in it, it is going to envelop that awful odor. First I opened it all up, removing drawers and dividers and let it set in the sun for an entire day. For the last 2 days I have been spraying it with a Febreeze like antibacterial spray 3 times a day. I don't really think I am making headway.
It is likely going to the closest donation center... OR... perhaps my husbands tool shop. Pretty fancy for nuts and bolts, but who cares if they stink? I always update my reviews if new info would be useful. In this case and at this time, I just cannot recommend it for purchase.
UPDATE: 11/13/2016.... Ultimately I was not able to remove the overwhelming odor emitted by the box. I felt it was so bad that I couldn't really take it to a donation center either. Husband said "No thanks" for use in his shop...so to the dumpster it went. I am disappointed. However, I will say that while I cannot recommend this box, the seller immediately contacted me following my review. They did refund my money in full, after first offering a replacement or $15.00 credit. The seller did also honestly communicate with me that the source all of the colors (3 to choose from) from the same manufacturer (so could be an issue with all of their stock) and the sellers were very fast with responses. While I still cannot recommend this particular product, I would say that the seller was very apologetic and actively sought a way to make me happy so the seller gets a higher rating that the product. I did also suggest that they check remaining stock, not sure if they plan to do or the ability to do so as the case may be stored and shipped from a location out of their immediate position. Sometimes is obviously wrong though. I will not likely update this review further as I no longer have this case.

On occasion I travel with too much to handle and so I thought this would help... Purchased for a beach themed baby shower gift... it was well received.  It looks a good quality and will wash and wear well.  If there are any issues to report I always update my reviews.  It arrived quickly ( I have prime) and in good condition.

I am 5'8", 38DDD and usually wear a 14/16 or 0x/1x... their "3x" fits like a snug 1x... just FYI. The black seems a bit thinner than the white... also the do not say Lucky 21 on the tag... it says "Zenana Signature"...  The material content is 95% cotton, 5% spandex. The V neck doesn't come down too far - but is not short in case that matters to you.  I will update if there is anything useful to report after washing.

I am 5''8"  38DDD, usually wear a 14/16 0/1x... these run a little small... so I ordered up to cover wash and dry... They are sleeveless, black, just as shown. So... I a 5'8" usually wear a 14/16 0x/1x.  First, I would say these run true to size.  Next, I am gonna call these really pink, not coral - in case that matters to you. The drawstring is functional.  No pockets. They are soft, but very thin.  I plan to use them to sleep in, so I'm ok with the thin part.  The hit me right about where they do on the model for length.
