Purchased these for my husband to wear when are traveling.  He is not comfortable in flip flops or other sandals and going through various securities, it is nice to have a shoe that is easy on/ easy off - even when you are TSA pre-check.  These fit the bill.  My husband is diabetic and has sensitive, wide feet with a couple of hammer toes. He basically uncomfortable in most shoes. Very challenging.  These are the first shoes we have purchased he has worn for hours and hours and not complained once.  I will say that while these Sketchers are the ultimate in comfort, they (both women's and men's) break down within 6 months or so with fairly regular wear, so we keep a few pairs on hand and recycle the old ones.  The older we get, the more important comfort is and these really work.  I do recommend that if you have not tried a pair on in person, it is a good idea to do so.  Each style and sometimes different fabrics fit a little differently I've found.  But once you have the general idea, you can order them fairly reliably online.  I always update if there are any wear or other issues that may be helpful.

I am 5'8"... 38DDD and usually wear a 14/16 or 0/1x.... I purchased this jacket purposely a size larger as I wanted it to fit loosely and for the arms not to be too tight when wearing long sleeves. I think it fits just as I wanted it too. It is also extremely light. Nice because classic denim can be very heavy and stiff until heavily worn. This jacket has body, but is stretchy too which makes is more comfortable.I also wanted the dark/indigo denim. The bulk of the jacket is dark, however, it is lighter in the front, like it's been "sand washed" in two strips... granted, it's subtle, but I wish it were solid. I am not a fan of the lighter, distressed look. It is also lighter across the shoulders and at the elbows. Perhaps an attempt to make it look more "worn." I washed it on "normal" (cold, regular spin) with Woolite for dark clothing and hung it to dry in my closet dryer, all no problem. The laundry directions say to lay flat to dry. I included a pic that is hopefully helpful. I put a 12" ruler on the jacket to sort of help you with the dimensions if you are considering size. All in all I like the jacket. Just wish they would not have "distressed" it. I always update if there are any issues or other important information to share.
UPDATE: 4/5/2016.... So I've washed the jacket a couple of times... it has done well. I do still wish it were not "distressed"... but it is a comfortable jacket to wear. It seems to hold it's size even after washing. I am still happy having gone up 1 size.

I own a dozen or so sketchers... each style fits a little bit differently so it's difficult to keep up with that... I found these to be a tad longer than others... but terribly so.  I love the leopard print that is subtle.  I would say you MIGHT be able to go down 1/2 a size in these... depends on how you like them to fit.  I find that after a few weeks they loosen up a bit too, although less so with the newer versions than the first generation.  I still love these slips-ons, for the most part, and will keep buying them.  I always update with any useful info.

So, I am 5'8"... 38DDD, usually wear a 14/16.  I have purchased Lucky many times and know that I am most comfortable in a 2x, so that is what I ordered.  Fits very loosely, but that's what I like.  The thing I didn't know what that this henley is pretty see through.  I included a pic wearing the top with a nude bra.. sooo.. .when I wear this shirt, I will most likely wear a black bra.  The sleeves are 3/4 length, with a button at the cuff.  They feel a bit snug to me.  I am just going to open it and push it up like the model shows.  The back of the blouse (also a pic) has open cut/embroidery area, all across the back shoulder area... from the neck down about 4 1/2".  However, the see through ribbing is throughout the entire body and sleeves of the blouse.  The tags say, Made in India... the body of the blouse is 80% vicose, 20% linen which answers why it is slightly scratchy and another reason why I also buy slightly large from Lucky - so I can wash and dry without worry.  The embroidery is listed as 100% cotton.  Washing:  Wash cold, tumble dry low, do not dry clean. The bottom line for me is that I love 99% of the clothes I buy from Lucky.  I would probably not buy this one again for a couple of reasons... don't really love how see through it is, it's a bit scratchy on your skin and the sleeve cuffs are a bit too tight. Just my opinion.  I always update if there are any issues that might be helpful.
Very thin... nearly see through... could be used as undershorts but they are questionable otherwise... won't be buying this mesh type again.

I have many pairs of Mayari style birkies... guess it's one of my favorite styles.  Oddly, I have never owned a black pair... so when I found this pair, I ordered them.  The leather tops are smooth, but not shiny... sort of like nubuck.  They are wee bit stiff, but not uncomfortably so and I know that once I've worn them for a few days, it will soften up.  Because I have owned birkies for years, I know that I can order the 39 and they fit perfectly and in 20 years that has not changed.  If you have never ordered or tried birkenstocks before, it would be good to try them on in person if you can... once you've nailed your size, it generally works with every pair you'll purchase, no matter the style.  Or at least that has been my experience.  I always update with any wear or other issues.

I'm 5'8", 38DDD and usually wear a 14/16. I am most comfortable going up 2 sizes with swimsuits and with this suit it was spot on. The print, navy/coral is beautiful... the trim and print is bright... it is a coral/pink - but to be honest, more pink than coral. I'm ok with that and it looks nice with the navy background. I have ordered this style swimsuit in other colors.. the black with white trim was disappointing because the black, when washed, turned the white trim gray... I washed and dried this color combo without any issues. The style is very flattering and comfortable. The top does not have underwire, but the cups are lightly foamed and there is about a 1" elastic band under the foam bra. The skirt doesn't really have tummy netting, but I find that it holds in my stomach a little bit. The top is not terribly long, but it's not cropped either. I like that the skort type skirt (shorts under the skirt), you can use it as a walking outfit too. It's just comfortable. I always update with any wear or other issues.
UPDATE: 2/1... the pic shows the inner bra area and the that the shorts are of the print. You can also see how pink the coral is. As I said, I think that it is definately a coral... but maybe more pink than coral. I like it!

I am 5'8"... 38DDD and usually wear a 14/16.... but I with Lucky Brand I know that I am most comfortable with a 2x as I prefer a little looser fit.  The back ground color is a darker ivory/beige... very lovely with the navy embroidered flower design.  It's perfect with jeans.  I also have a pair of capri pants that are the beige color in shirt and I think it will work with those for summer because the blouse is a little longer.  It has a tie at the neck, but I prefer to just leave it open.  It's not low at all so it's comfortable.  I machine washed and tumble dried with no issues at all.  Honestly, I find Lucky Brand usually to be very good quality and lasts as long as I am interested in wearing them.  I always update with any wear or other issues.

I am 5'8"... 38DDD and usually wear a 14/16... I am familiar with Lucky Brand and know that I am most comfortable in their 2x... granted it is very loose... but I prefer that. If I were going to wear it out, I might put a nude cami underneath... or not... I don't think a bra showing is that shocking or unacceptable... personal choice. The graphic is light in color.. muted. I like it. I machine washed and tumble dried, no trouble. Easy, comfortable wear. I always update with any wear or other issues that could be helpful.
UPDATE: 2/22/2016.... Ok... this t seems have gotten even more see through with washing... Ok for me.. I am sort of using it as a night shirt... but just in case that might be an issue for any one out there... It's pretty shear and appears to becoming more so with each wash... took away one star..

I am 5'8"... 38DDD.. usually wear a 14/16.. The 1x fit perfectly... I am 5'8" 38DDD... I usually wear a 14/16 or 1x... With Lucky brand I know that I am most comfortable with a 2x.  I like a looser fit.  This blouse is a nice dark navy with an ivory and gold color medallion type shapes... There is a tie at the neck.  The sleeves are are cap and flutter.  The photo shows the blouse tucked into the models pants (which is sort of weird), can be misleading that the shirt is shaped this way.  It is the same length in the front and back.
