I needed a very lightweight carryon. I always buy the black and then I stencil with personalized print of my choice - using the oil based sharpie pens in gold and silver. This bag spins on it's wheels easily. I have not had the tip over issues mentioned in other reviews, but as one comment mentioned, if this happens, you just need to turn the wheels 90 degrees or so. With any case that has spinner wheels tip over is possible if they line up just right. The inside is surprisingly large and I find it easily holds what I need for a long weekend. It has straps inside to hold one side in place. There are lots of zippered compartments outside to access papers or other items you might need to... notepad, even a laptop. Just depends on how stuffed you want the bag to be. I like to keep it pretty thin in order to now have to fight it in or out of the overhead. Flying Alaska and United most of the time I have not had any issues with overhead stowing. The handle seems sturdy. I always update with any issues that might be helpful.
UPDATE: 1 31 2016.... I've added a few pics to better show the case inside and out....Also, I failed to mention that this case also offers an "extension" zip giving you about 1" or so once opened. Remember: I personalize my black bags so I can easily spot them - when you order the bag, it will be plain black until you release your inner crafty person! Happy travels!

Well... I clearly OVER estimating the sizing... haha... I am 5'8" and usually buy a 14/16.  For some reason I was under the impression this ran really small reading other reviews and looking at photos.. well... The blouse is so large it's a dress!  There is no tightness at the bottom...  It's very soft and so it's now become a nightie, which is fine too.  The tag is 95% rayon, 5% spandex.  The only wash directions say to wash with like colors.  I would recommend delicate cycle and line or flat dry due the content.  I always update with any issues.  In this case I would carefully research sizing!

I buy ruanas for traveling... lightweight but warm and easy to carry or rollup.  I am 5'8" and usually buy a 14/16.  This ruana fits very loosely as ruanas generally do.  The back of the ruana falls below my rump in the back.  There is a fringe, as shown in the photo, which I generally avoid, but I liked the pattern so I tried it.  The fringe is about 3 1/2" inches long and each strand appears to be 2 strands twisted together.  The tag says 100%  acrylic and spot clean only.  I think it may be because of the fringe... although it is acrylic which technically you can wash... I always update if there are any issues.

I like ruanas for travel.... lightweight, but warm. Easy to carry or roll up and store. I am 5'8" and usually buy a 14/16.  This ruana is very soft... there is a fringe around the bottom...about 1 inch long.  When wearing it it hits me at about mid rump in the back, so it's a shorter ruana. I did have to clip quite a few threads - caused one star in rating.. It is a dark charcoal, a nice color for jeans or slacks. The fabric content tag says: 100% acrylic...so it's not as warm as wool, but not scratchy either.  care says: hand wash, lay flat to dry, low iron if desired.  I always update with any issues.

I am 5'8".. 38DDD and usually buy a 14/16, 0x or 1x. I have purchased this brand before and know that a 2x works best for me. This blouse was no exception in sizing. I read the the other reviews and I've included a picture. I took the pic with natural daylight coming from behind me. I have a "nude" bra on. Honestly, it is a little see through, but I don't feel inclined to have a wear a cami underneath. I think it is a personal choice. The blouse is well designed to be flattering on a lot of different body types. A few words on the design... the strip under the breast portion is very snug... but it is positioned in such a way that it is too empirish. The bottom of the flutter panel in the front of the blouse is actually sewn down, so that reduces the look of a maternity top. I also like that the v front is not too low. You can actually manipulate the v a little to make it even less revealing... or more... your choice. The sleeves are nice and loose and hit me at the elbow, as shown on the model. The material is very soft. Fabric content: 95% rayon 5% spandex... wash instructions say: hand wash cold, with like colors. Do not bleach. Lay flat to dry, low iron when needed. I wash in my machine on delicate/handwash and I have a dryer "closet" that I actually hang it to dry with a "shake" cycle... so I don't need to iron. I also bough this blouse in black. I am really glad I did. Both could be dressed "up" or worn with jeans. I think they will both be good, versatile pieces. I always update with any issues that might be helpful.
UPDATE: I LOVE these blouses. Very, very well designed for the larger woman. They washed up perfectly and I've worn the black one on a plane trip I recently took. Heavenly comfort and yet.. with style. Love love love.

I am 5'8" and usually buy a 14/16 ... This brand runs a bit large, however, I have big thighs so I ordered the "large"....  fits me perfectly.  The drawstring is "working" and there are pockets. The navy is a sort of lighter navy.  The material is soft and very comfortable, machine wash/tumble dry.

I missed the part that said these were "bifocals" and not full lens correction.  So I am disappointed.  I will keep them, but probably pass them to my husband.  I use the full lens for when I am not wearing my contacts, so it matters to me.  The glasses themselves seem pretty good quality... there are rubber tips on the area that rests over the ears for comfort... I have an average size head and they fit nicely, not loose, but they would "stretch" to fit a larger size head... but they are not hinged.  The actual bifocal area is quite small. You need to be looking way down.The lens are a medium gray/black and have a sticker that says UV400 on them.  Also note that the word "Aloha" is on the top left lens in script - in case that matters to you. They come with a large, black hard case with an orange zipper and a cleaning cloth... a nice plus.  I always update with any issues or other useful info.

I normally buy a 38DDD but that was not available so I ordered this one at 40D... it is bigger around, obviously but can work at the tightest hook point.  The cups are large enough... there is rubber stripping on the top of the cups to help keep the cups in place as they are not foam cupped, instead there are double material layers to form the cup.  There is a pretty strong wire on the under cup. It will work for a strapless bra but it is not as comfortable as I would like and I would have preferred the cups be slightly foam filled so the cups would hold there shape better. The "nude" is a lighter nude if that matters to you, about the same as most of my "nude" bras.  I always update if there are any issues or other useful info.

I accidently ordered 2 sizes bigger than I'd planned, but it actually is ok... the shorts themselves are wide legged... the top is high waisted which is good for a flattering, no muffin top look.  The tie is functioning too.  I wish I could have purchased in black, this blue is a bit deeper than the photo shows in my opinion, however I found a couple of swimtops with blue and black in them I think will work. Machine washed and tumble dried nicely.  I do recommend using a lingerie bag so the tie doesn't get tangled with other items or inside your machine if you have an older top loader.  I always update with any issues or other useful info.

I love this top.. although I have to say that I needed the 18... The cups are not quite big enough for me.  However, it is a very flattering cut and style for a lot of different body styles.  It's fairly loose on the bottom portion of the top and I think it's a bit longer than shown in the photo.  The straps are adjustable.  Could be worn with some capri pants I think.  Not too low in the front, although there is a little cleavage visible if that matters to you.  If you're not as full chested, probably won't be much.  Machine wash and tumbled dry beautifully.  I always update with issues or other helpful info.

These are my husband's favorite cotton blend short.  He wears them all the time.  From my perspective, they wash and wear well.  Machine wash, tumble dry - color fast. They hit him just about exactly as they do on the photo model, just about at the knee.  They are what I would say are a medium weight... not terribly light or heavy.  I always update with any issues.

I purchased this as a gift for my son in law, but in eyeballing it, it appears to be a pretty average size large in men's apparel.  The print is nice and the tshirt felt soft to me.  I always update with any issues and I will with this too.

Husband's medic alert tag is black. I ordered several different chain lengths and this one in black.  He chose it out the pile of chains to chose from and likes it the best.  I always update with any issues in wear or other.

These are a little bit of a hassle to use initially as you are fighting to quickly slide your card inside of them... but once you've used them a few times, it's way easier.  My husband over stuffed his passport sleeve and it tore.  So I highly recommend not to force too much in them.  This set comes with so many spare credit card sleeves you can share... unless you have a boat load of credit cards.  Only 2 passport sleeves.  I will have to find a source to buy just a couple of those instead of a whole new set of these with sleeves I won't need.  They arrived quickly (I have prime) and in good condition.  I always update if there are any issues.

I purchased 2 of these to wear together on my thumb.  I have to admit that I wasn't expecting much... but honestly these bands are pretty awesome. And strong.  The rhodium plating really makes them look like white gold or possibly even platinum - which I think rhodium is part of (?).  I pinched them between my fingers with a pretty good amount of pressure and they didn't budge. So they seem pretty solid and strong.  It appears they will be able to take the "pressure" that thumb rings get!  Even though these rings have "texture" it is smooth and did not snag or catch when I tested them.  They arrived quickly and in good condition.  I will be updating if there are any issues in wear or other to report.  In my opinion, well worth the $7 (ring + shipping - more than ring cost itself).

UPDATE: 6/20/2016.... Sadly, a stone popped out. Had hoped this would last at least 6 mos. :( It was very comfortable to wear... BUT it did snag things on the settings... and of course, with the stone gone, it's a cheap looking piece of junk jewelry - no longer "believable" in way shape or form. Eh. I HATE to update reviews when it's no good. I do NOT recommend this piece any longer. Sorry....
On first blush, this ring seems very worth the less than 10 bucks (ring + shipping - actually more than the ring cost). It is comfortable to wear so far. I was nervous that the double band effect would be uncomfortable between fingers, however, I quickly got used to the ring and don't notice it at all now. The back of the ring narrows just the right amount to make bending and clutching your hand easy, comfortable. Also, the bands themselves seems strong... able to take a bit of pressure without bending - although I don't recommend a stress test to try the theory. Just pinching them between my fingers with some pretty good strength didn't do anything to the bands. The stones are surprisingly sparkly considering how tiny they are. I have to say, however, it is a bit rough over the stone area due to the stone setting, so I would use caution wearing it with fine silks, sweaters and other snaggy material. The plating seems bright and I think it looks like a lot more than the price paid in my opinion. Definitely "believable" as white gold and real diamonds - however, it is plated silver and CZ's just to be clear.
I will be updating should there be any wear or other issues to report. It arrived quickly and in good condition.
I was very surprised to get this brush and how small the head is ... about 1 1/2 inches... the handle makes it easy to use.  I have used the brush side and worked well.

Holland America Cruise tags works perfectly with these.  Shared with family members also taking the cruise.  They will be able to be reused and definitely better than stapling paper to your bag that could be easily torn off.  I recommend these if you cruise!  If there are any issues to report I will update.

This purse is nearly an exact replica of my favorite I've had for about 15 years. As my old one has aged I keep saying, I just need a couple more inches all the way around and this bag would be perfect. (Ha - that's what she said - I know)... My old bag looked like a huge balloon ready to pop after I have everything I need in it - I've reduced as much as I can.... It says that it is genuine leather and removable tags on the bag also say this. The texture is very smooth... To be honest, it doesn't really feel like real leather... but who knows? The texture is so perfect, not usually the case with leather - there is always a flaw somewhere... on the flip side, perhaps this is just a really fine quality. I am hoping that it is real and will become as supple as my old bag with some wear and use. The stitching seems really good. I also hated the skinny strap of my old bag... the new bag has a lovely strap that is about an 1" in width... for me, just right - and so much more comfortable.... but everyone has a personal preference. I like that I can shoulder it or crossover, which I do when I need my hands free - doesn't bother my back as much as just shouldering it. There is plenty of adjustment on the strap for the hang length as well... I am about 5'8" and there was spare length at about low belly/high hip. When you open the flap there are a couple of magnetic envelope pouches, the largest fits my new LG G4 perfectly, three slots and a zippered area. In the main body of the bag there are two zippered organizers and a couple of side slots, that would hold a very small cell phone, keys fobs or other things you want to reach quick - too short for reading glasses, though... The bag itself isn't terribly heavy which is good. When I "load" it with everything I need it's still reasonably weighted... 3-5 lbs (?) It came with a storage/dust bag, a nice touch, although it will probably not spend much time in it. It arrived quickly (I have prime) along with some other things I had ordered. I will be updating with any wear or other issues as necessary. I will try to include some pics if they get past the censors and they include my old bag for reference as well as a 12" ruler for comparison.
8/27/2015... I have been using this bag exclusively for a few weeks. It's STILL my fav. I should also add that if you wanted to you could slide in a kindle or an IPAD if you wanted to. I have the larger Kindle and when I am going somewhere I know I will be waiting, I slide it in. I am seriously considering buying a second one to have a backup when this one gives out, hopefully years from now. :) Could go with a different color... but honestly I usually buy the black as it goes with everything, all year. But that's just me. I did get a like smudge of something grayish/white. I used a little leather cleaner and wiped it off, a very slight amount of black come off too, but didn't leave any noticeable trace. So I am ok with that. It has NOT left any color on my clothing. And I have been in damp area with rain/mist and/or sweaty humidity - so... I think that is good. I will continue to update with any issues or other thoughts.

I have several old eagle creek travel shoe bags (5+ ? years old) and I was so excited to get some additional bags!  As is often the case with "new" versions, there have been some changes... sadly the new ones (these) seem to be thinner, less durable material, feels less rubbery to me than the older version.... and the handles are thinner, more narrow, less heavy duty braid as well.  We'll see. I love this style and will use them. Initially I thought they were a tad smaller, but when I flattened them out and compared with an old style, they may be slightly smaller, but not really enough to notice.  There is enough room for one pair of larger style shoes (big heels/wedges) or three or four pairs of flipflops or maybe two pairs of flats with shoe tops...depending on the size of your shoe (mine are generally 8 to 9) There is a zipper, which, again, seems less durable but I remain hopeful. I like that my shoe bottoms don't touch my clothes as I am traveling. And with the zipper, the shoes stay in the bag as opposed to drawstring style.  If color is important to you, they are a darkish gray, zipper and handle is black.  They arrived quickly (I have prime) and in great condition with some other items I ordered. I will update if there are any wear or other issues to report.

First things first.... I am about 5'8".. usually wear a 14/16, 38DDD and my body shape is a curvy hour glass.  This robe is a beautiful, rich, deep navy and very, very thin. The tie is sewn on.  There are pockets on both sides.  There is an inside tie as well to help keep the robe closed.  However, I will say that it is very, very low cut even when tied, in case that matters to you.  The material is very soft... however, SUPER thin... glad I didn't go with one of the lighter colors. You kind of want a robe to be a bit more coverage - or at least I do. I bought it to take when I travel because it's lightweight (and it is) and easy to wash, which it appears to be.  I gentle washed it and very low temp dried it.  I would say that it is a bit smaller than I would like particularly on the top, but that is usually a problem I have and don't necessarily hold that against the robe.  Wish there was a bit more material on top and that it stayed closed a bit better.  Probably would not buy again.

I have plantar fasciitis. These have a slight heel which is better for those of who suffer from this condition than a purely flat flip flop. I wear an 8 1/2-9 in regular shoes... Usually an 8 in crocs. I also have two different sizes of feet due to polio - almost 2 sizes. What I am going to say is that it may be a good idea to size up on this style. with the rubber top and the toe separator it does "pull" a bit at the toe and the top of the foot. The foot bed is wide... but I am ok with that. I don't like my foot to slop over or my toes to curl over the top of the end of the shoe. The strap is adjustable and that helps with the tightness over the top of the foot... but with the nature of the rubber type material, it still tends to "grab" your skin on the top of your foot. I like the look of the shoe. I like the slight heel. I am not so fond of the pulling between the toes. I am going to see if I get used to it. The size 8's I ordered came from a different seller of the size 9's or location and came in a USPS envelope. If there are any wear issues or defects, I will update.
8/27/2015... Ok... after trying for several weeks to wear these, I am donating them. The toe center just pulls too much and is uncomfortable between my toes. I am not reducing my stars... because I think it is a personal choice... some may find it just fine. Everyone's toes are different.

I bought this for my Mom to wear with an evening gown on a cruise.  She is sensitive to "tight" things around her head, like headbands, etc... so I thought this might be a good alternative. It's is not stretchy, so therefore not tight and adjustable at the back in regards to drop and fit.  It is a silver color metal...made in china. As others have noted, you probably don't want to sweat too much wearing it as it may turn your skin green or other color?  It is an unknown base metal.  The rhinestones are a slightly sparkly, but not like a quality cz would be..  I think appropriate for the price point.  This is NOT a high dollar item, nor does it look like one at close inspection, however, I believe it will work for the one time wear planned. The photo represents the style and what I received well. It arrived in a bubble pouch via usps in good time and in good condition. I will update with any issues.

Ooo.. these are beautiful... black base with a coral/pink pink/design.  I love them.  I do recommend ordering a full size larger than you ordinarily do with all Ipanemas.  In fact, I think they recommend it on their sale pages.  In any case, I usually wear an 8 1/2 -  9.  The 10 in the Ipanemas is perfect.  As flip flops go, these last several seasons.  And I usually wear them exclusively through the season, granted I own several different color options.  If you haven't tried a pair, I say roll the dice and try just one pair.  Everyone is different, but honestly these are the most comfy/stylish flips I own.  Dress up or down.  You choose.

I own many Ipanema flip flops and I was looking for a black/silver print to replace one they don't make any more.... These are ok... they are black and silver... however, the base (the foot bed) of the flip is a bit slippery with a slight texture.  Not the grabby "cush" style of my other pair. Different material I guess...  I have to look more carefully at the pics to be sure I am buying the type I like.  These will be ok.. but not the "go-to's" of my other pairs.  The other thing to know is that you must go up one full size when ordering Ipanemas.  I normally wear an 8/12 -9.  The 10 in the Ipanema fits me perfectly - I don't like my heel to hang over the edge on my flip flops, nor do I like a lot of space behind when when wearing a size too large.  And the 10 is perfect match.  I wear my Ipanema's almost exclusively during the season and they have lasted 3 or so seasons.  I think that is impressive for a flip-flop. I will of course update if there are any issues.

I own a number of Ipanema flip flop and have been trying to find a black/silver replacement for a print they no longer make... This one is close.  Not as bright silver on the straps, but it's pretty.  The top of the flips is sort of a dark chocolate bronzy color on top of a black bottom... I was expecting something more totally black.. but they aren't bad.  The bases are similar in "cushion" and I like that.  I will say that you definitely want to buy a full size up from what you normally buy.  I usually wear an 8 1/2 -9... the 10 in the Ipanema is the perfect length.  I don't like my heel right on the edge of of the flip flop, but I don't like a huge space at the back of my foot either.  I believe they even suggest you size up on their pages.  I wish my previous print was still available, but these will be ok for now.

I own so many pairs of these slipon Skechers.  I love them all. For the review I would say that they fit to size... not too small or too large.  I will say that they do tend to stretch a bit after you've worn them for months.. I find this only makes them easier to put on.  I have an average width foot and find this very comfortable across the top - not too tight, not too loose.  However, everyone's foot is different, so I would honestly suggest you try on a pair to find just the right fit.  It was easy when there was only one "type"... now they have so many different styles and I find they each fit differently.  This particular style features "memory foam" soles and they are particularly comfortable because of that.  They feel like you're wearing a really high quality slipper around.  Very lightweight.  Very soft.  I am waiting for them to come out with a really lovely coral color!  I do like that these black shoes do not have the light gray stitching as accent.. they are all black, including the soles.  Now the inside is a really vibrant neon red/pink.  But you don't notice that when your foot is in.  Hope this helps in some way.

Bought these for my Mom when she purchased the same shoe in red at a local store.  She liked the shoe well enough (and that is NOT easy) that she wanted other colors and chose this silver.  Mom has an average foot, maybe even a bit to the more narrow in my opinion.  These are nicely adjustable and are comfortable enough for her to wear all day.  The quality is good and I believe she will have these long past the time she would want them.

As reported by many others, this robe runs very small.  I normally order a 14/16, 1x.  I ordered the 3x which fits like a 1x... sort of. The under arms are really huge.  Also, it's like it was a "second".... there was a "tie" on one side of the robe (meant to secure to another tie on the inside, keeping the robe closed) BUT the inner tie was not there.  Disappointing.  The outer tie is sewn on as reported by others and is not too high for me... I am 5'8".  The length hits me right at the knees.  I am going to keep it as I plan to use as a swim suit cover up but it is not a really awesome robe.

This style of Birkenstock is my fav.  But I have to admit I missed in the pic that it was patent "shiny" white and not leather tops.  :(  Was hoping for a matte leather.  I am not sending them back as white is difficult to get in this style.  But I will be much more careful in examining the look of each in the photos, ask questions if I am not sure....  They are also very, very lightweight.  In fact more light that others I have in my collection.  Looking on the bright side of the shine, perhaps it will be easier to clean?? I will update if there are any issues.

I actually purchased these at my local Nordstrom's because I must buy 2 different sizes and they will split them at NO cost to me. Having said that, this was my first purchase of Gentle Souls and I have to say that I LOVE them.  I purchased this style in black and the silver/white as a dressier but no heel shoe.  They are perfect.  My feet are super hard to fit, not just because I have nearly 2 size difference between my feet... always a challenge and mostly I end up disappointed.  They are soft, cushy, but not too foamy and are soooo comfortable I can wear them ALL day.  Nothing rubs... nothing pokes out.. nothing snags....In a word... perfect. They are stylish without looking like old lady shoes and go with a casual dress or a pair of jeans or capris as easily.  Honestly, I am eager to begin exploring other styles they offer.  I am TOTALLY sold on this style and on this company.  I did NOT find that they ran "large" or wide or narrow.  I have an average foot and found them to be perfect in length and width - but everyone's feet are so unique.  I think that if you're looking for a pair of flats and really NEED them to comfortable, don't hesitate to at least try these.

Ok... I am 5'8" usually wear a 14/16 and buy an 18 in swimwear.  On a hunch, I decided to go up one further size and I it was the right hunch.  The waist fits perfectly... not too loose, not to snug.  The waist band is wider than I have had on other swim shorts (about 1") and I find this very comfortable. They are high-waisted, well above the belly button, again, I like that.  Less muffin top.  The inner pantie holds you in a bit... but not too tightly, will smooth some lumps and rolls. The legs are wide, but not too loose.  The are longer, hitting me high mid thigh.  They washed up nicely. When wet, they don't cling too tightly to my leg which I like. Overall I believe they are well made and one of my favorites.  I will update my post if there are any wear issues as I use them.

I am 5'8" and usually wear a 14/16, body shape is a curvy hour glass. These fit me perfectly and what a perfect pant for travel... no wrinkling. Easy wash and dry. Dress it up or casual/playful.  These are are my go to's for just about everywhere.  In fact, I bought 2 pairs in plain black to go with the many others I have in various prints.  The are true wide leg palazzo style. No pockets for those who care.  If you haven't tried a pair, you should!  Be mindful of their sizing chart, it will help you chose just the right size.  Happy fancy pants!

Ok... here's the stats... I am 5'8" tall... usually wear a 14/16, my body shape is a curvy hourglass.  These fit me perfectly.  I have to report that I shared them with my sister who is 6' and a similar but longer body and legs than I.  They fit her a bit better for length.  I will say that the pants I received had quite a bit more yellow flowers than the photo really shows...  but she loves them.  The navy tank she happened to have on while trying them on was actually a good match.  I own several pairs from this seller and I would advise that you follow their sizing info.  These are just perfect for traveling as I do, no wrinkles and lightweight.  I do throw them in a gentle machine wash and tumble dry at very low or just hang them to dry.  If you haven't tried these, you're missing out!  A great look for many body types and if you travel as much as I do, the perfect fun/casual/dress it up pant!

Bought these to go with Mom's bright butterfuly luggage. A great match.  They are pretty thick and have these SUPER strong, metal cable attachments. I think they are going to be "stuck" on the luggage for a long time.

I am 5'8" and usually wear a 14/16.  I heard once, buy 1 size up from your regular clothes. I did, but I found that I could not get them up as high as shown in the photo, which is what I wanted and they were a bit snug around the waist. So, I decided to go for one more size up just to see. Perfect.  Fortunately they did not make it much bigger in the waist and they were just the right height. The legs are nice and wide and could be easily worn as casual shorts. I bought black and brown to mix with my various tankini tops. I really like them!

I am 5'8", 38DDD and wear a 14/16.  I am bigger on top than bottom so I like to buy separates. On this particular top, the black and white is very bright and crisp.  It is a flattering look that you could wear with a pair shorts or capri's or other pant as well as swim bottom.  It is nicely scooped so there isn't a ton of cleavage, which I like.  However, it is a bit big under the arms.  The straps are adjustable, but only to a point, because it will pull under the arm it you hike the straps up too far. No underwire, so less lift, but more comfort!  I like it.  I probably will wear only on land, as if it gets wet, it may just slip off!  Dry I believe it will be fine!

I purchased these as a gift for a friend and she reports they are extremely comfortable, fit perfectly and are fine to wear all day.  She is a plus size person with a normal to maybe slightly wider foot... She loves them.

If you travel a fair amount of the time, these are great space savers.  I have used the vacuum bags a lot and I find that it is also handy to store things at home as well as for travel.  These I purchased for my best friend to help her pack for a trip we are planning.  I think they are a thoughtful gift for travelers and maybe college students?  I will update my review if there are any quality or other issues.

Bought as an accessory for a luggage set I purchased for my best friend of 30 years... we are taking a trip to Maui together later this year and I thought it would be fun to have new luggage and tags.  She reports these are large and easy to see... making it easy pick out on the luggage conveyor.  Cool and functional.  Awesome.

Purchased as a surprise gift for my best friend of 30 years... we are taking a trip to Maui this year and she loves red.  She reported that she was THRILLED with the color/print and that it arrived in very good condition, all pieces packed inside the next so initially she didn't realize there were so many pieces!  She was thrilled to have all the choices and that they are so easy to move around with the 360 rollers.  She reports that they look like a very high quality set.  Sometimes these sets look and feel cheap, no so in this case, per her report.  She says that it is the perfect set for a short weekend trip - using the smaller bags, or a month long trip - use them all.  I would say this set is a winner all around.

Purchased for my best friend (of thirty years) - always a risk to buy clothes for others, but I THINK I know a little bit about her... anyway... her initial report was that it was a bit on the large side... but her daughters report that it fit her perfectly and is a "loose" fit look.  She loves the colors and says the picture is a very good representation of the colors.  Just be prepared that this blouse is meant to be a loose, comfortable fit... not fitted.  Best friend is 5"5... usually an 18/20 and about 40B if that helps you compare to your fit.

I bought some red print luggage for a friend (my best friend of more than 30 years) to take to Maui on a trip we are taking together... I thought the red TSA locks were a perfect match.  (Her favorite color is red)  The other side is of this is that the red color stands out.. it is easy to see and access.  These particular locks have a three number lock - I actually prefer the 4 digit option, but these work.

I first found this artist on Maui and I love all the matching pieces!  Some of the pieces are dark in color I chose this one for the brighter colors and the sentiment.  It is larger than it appears and is very handy in the matching hand bag.

I first found this artist on Maui and I love all the matching pieces!  Some of the pieces are dark in color I chose this one for the brighter colors and the sentiment.  The great part of this bag is the functionality.  It's REALLY handy to throw your smaller purchases right in your bag and sort out when you get home. This possible because the bag it's self is quite light. On one side there are pockets on the other side a zippered pouch. I have a galaxy 5 and it fits nicely in the larger pouch (non zippered), my reading glasses in the other, or sunglasses. The bag has a strong magnetic close and stays closed nicely. It also nice that it opens WIDE to quickly find what you need in the bottom. If you get something on it, it easily wipes off, even inside of the silky material. I own a LOT of handbags...and this maker is quickly overtaking all my others. I will definitely be following this artist and her bags. I just love it and it never fails to attract many admirers.
I first found this artist on Maui and I love all the matching pieces!  Some of the pieces are dark in color I chose this one for the brighter colors and the sentiment.

I bought this for my husband as a gift.  It is stainless steel and is heavy.  He really thinks the magnetic properties help him to feel more energetic.. I'm not sure about all that.  But it is a really nice looking bracelet. He is a bigger guy and it fits him perfectly. He doesn't like a lot of extra "play" in the length so this works.  I will update if there are any wear or other issues.

For your comparison, I am 5'8", 38DD/DDD and usually wear a 14/16.  I purchased the "slate"... it's really more of a taupe to me... (a gray/brown, more gray than brown tones) and I like it.  It is 100% acrylic, made in China and lists a preferred handwash and dry flat... although I think a gentle/delicate machine wash would be fine.  It drapes nicely and has those very handy pockets for your cell phone or tissue.  I plan to wear it often this Fall/Winter.  In fact, I am going to consider other colors if they are available.

My husband lived in and worked in Carhatts practically his whole life.  The brand is recognized for tough wear, long life, protection and he would definitely agree. My husband is a big guy (3x), but we typically buy one whole size larger for ease of movement.  He wishes they would offer everything they make in extended sizes. The box arrived virtually open, with only one strand of reinforced tape intact, however, the inner plastic bag the jacket was in was not open and all looked fine.

Yup.  They are.  Granny panties.  But at least they aren't baggy.  The legs would be better if high cut.  I had some surgery, so I needed a bit more coverage for a time.  I'm pretty sure these won't be my favs, but they will work.  Easy wash.  The "sand" is a light "nude"....I would watch and grab them when the price drops.  In my opinion the sizing is right.

I have had many pairs of Teva over the years and I just wanted this color. You will want to order one size up. I like that the foot bed is black... with lighter colors they really show dirt... yuck.. They do have a bumpy texture which does take a little getting used to if you're used to flat, smooth flip-flop surfaces.  In my experience they last years and years without breaking down, which is nice.  They usually cost a little more than most flip-flops.  There is a slight heel and arch, but it's not really orthotic in any way, however, probably better than a purely flat foot bed.

I have many swim tops and like to change out the bottoms. I liked these because they go high enough... above the belly button... They have a panty underneath that helps to hold you in some which is nice and the leg opening is generous, not tight.  I am 5'8" usually wear a 14/16.  I always buy one size up in swimsuits and this was a good choice.  The appear to be well made and I think they will last a long time.  I will update if I have any issues with wear, etc.  I will look to see if these shorts come in some different colors too.

Every year I buy silly/useful things for Christmas gifts and I purchased this one for my brother who is an avid runner.  I hope that the L/XL will work and won't be too big.  But then he has a big head and big brain. HA.  Safety first. It arrived quickly and in good shape.  I will update with any issues.

Ok... first, I think this seller's products run large... secondly, the color is so far removed from the photo.  It's WAY brighter... just awful really.  It looks like a sack on.  I am 5'8", wear a 14/16 and 38DD/DDD.. which is why I sometimes purchase a little larger.  However, it is not necessary with this dress... it was HUGE under the arms.  Even when I pinched it in the back, it still looked like a sack.  I think this may be a dress better suited to someone very slender?  I am returning it.

I am 5'8" and usually wear a 14/16 with a classic hour glass type shape and purchase the size 8. I don't like panties too tight.  I found these panties to fit me perfectly.  They don't ride up.  They stay where they should and no tags.  I have replaced all my others with these.  I buy them when they reach a certain price point from Amazon and/or at the store when they are on sale for between $5-6.50 each.  They machine wash and tumble dry easily.  Have them in both black and nude.  Not too tight, but they don't slip  down. Honestly, purchasing panties is a very personal thing, but I think if you try one pair, you'll be sold too.

I ordered two. Shipping is a little slow, but they arrived in good condition. First, these bags are huge. I weighed them with my luggage hook and it said 9 pounds, just as advertised. Lots of neat features. The wheels are protected by little edges and reinforced corners, so they may last a bit longer when battered by the baggage handlers. There is a extender zip, but honestly they are so huge inside I can't imagine using it. As I was unpacking them I discovered there is a handle on the bottom where the wheels are! What a brilliant idea. This means you can grab this suitcase just about any way it lands. SUPER handy when you're chasing your bag around the carousel because it landed wheel side toward you. There was a bit of chemical smell when I opened them, but I just aired them out and it is greatly reduced. I might lightly mist them with some Febreeze. But only if the smell lingers. The zippers are very heavy duty and the main zips do have the lock loop. Inside there is a flat, top zip area and two adjustable straps on the bottom main compartment with a small side, mesh zip bag that is attached. Pretty standard confirguration inside. There is a limited warranty for manufacturer defect only and NO airline or consumer abuse and/or normal wear issues.
I did purchase the basic black because they were cheaper, but here's a tip: I use permanent sharpie pens (gold, silver and copper - sold on Amazon for about $5.99 a set of three) and a stencil (also on Amazon and themes to fit everyone out there... at about $7.00 a sheet) to personalize my suitcases so they are quick and easy to spot on the baggage carousel.. even at a distance. Because they are uniquely decorated, no one ever confuses my bag for theirs. Win/Win.
Tag inside one bag said "Designed in the USA, made in China" Thought about docking one star for that part....
I travel a lot and will update if there are any wear or other issues.
UPDATE 6/15/2014: The chemical smell is a bit more than I'd like... doing the Febreeze and I think a day out in the sun. Some might consider a "new car" type smell, however, I am sensitive to chemical smells in general.

I wanted a shorter, (meaning not full length) robe for after my showers and/or easy morning lounging.  I am 5'8"  38DD/DDD and usually wear a 14/16.  This robe fits loosely but comfortably so and hits about my knees. The material is super soft, machine wash/dry.  The coral color is well represented in the photo, perhaps a bit richer in person.  I plan to go back and see what other color options there are, but may just purchase a second coral robe.  The zipper is good quality and the robe appears to be well made.  I will update if there are any issues after washing and/or wear.

I can't rate this product less because it didn't work for me... I LOVED it. However, I am 38DD/DDD, 5'8" and wear a 14/16 regularly. The top was just not enough cup coverage for my shape, showing breast under the arm. :( So the coverage was just not enough. The style is terrific. If is does work for you in the cup, it is very deeply cut, so not for the shy at heart. The material print was really unique and I am guessing it would wash/wear/pack beautifully. I really REALLY wanted this work... but sadly I had to return both.. I ordered the pink in XL and the green in L... Was thinking the "green" was turquoise by the photos, but you should know it is really a kelly green. I prefered the vivid pink.
Would have loved it... but had to return.

My husband is 3x type guy that works in cold areas on ocassion. He's purchased carhartts for more than 30 years and knows they always run small so he opted for 2 sizes bigger than normal in the insulated bibs and,as it turns out, the largest size carhartt offers. Sadly they were still at least 2 sizes too small! He was soooo disappointed to have to return these and purchase an brand other than carhartt. Wish Carhartt would review their sizing and offer a bit more of the larger sizes AND more accurate sizing information.
As I said... sadly, these were returned. :(

I've purchased many of these "cover ups" from this seller and I have to say that I really like this one.  I use them as tunics over skinny jeans. Most of the others I have purchased have waist cinches, but this one has elastic sewn in the sides and back.  It is an "empire" look for me... I am 5'8", usually wear a 14/16 and 38DD/DDD.  The material is poly blend that hangs nicely and washes nicely too. It hits me mid/high thigh.  I purchased this one to travel with me when I go to Islands over Christmas (thus the red)... I will say that it is a really, really deep pink rather than a true red which is ok with me.  The edges are so nicely trimmed with a cotton crochet.  Very feminine.  I plan to wear it with white skinny jeans or maybe even shorts.  I own about 8 or so of these tunic type "cover ups" and will continue to buy them from this seller.  Hope this helps!

These sandals are comfortable and BRIGHT.  VERY electric!  The photos are accurate I think.  Just be aware that they are not subtle.  I personally really like pink and orange combined and so I purchased these.  They are comfortable with the cloth strap between the toes and padded foot bed. The "ribs" on the footbed are really not noticeable to me. Because of the material, I think they will be easily hosed off as necessary. Living at the beach, I rinse of sand regularly. You can see me coming a mile away with the bright color. Wish they were a shade or two lighter, but I'm keeping em!

First, be sure to mind the sizing chart for Roamans.  If Amazon isn't showing their size chart go to roamans.com.  They run VERY big even for plus sizes.  I ordered the large knowing it would be loose.  I will keep it... but probably would go for the medium is I were to order again.  I am 5'8"  38DD/DDD.  I usually wear a 14/16. It is a pretty cardigan.  I ordered the white and it is a bright white.  Oddly they sewed a black tag at the neck, so I removed that. The crochet is pretty and I like the scalloped edge. There is a good sized hook/eye clasp at the point of the neck and the hook/eye is white. Also, the model is obviously NOT a "plus" size model. Therefore the sleeves are not as long on the arm as the picture shows, but they are plenty long enough for me, ending about the elbow. Just not three-quarter length.  It says hand wash and reshape/lay flat.  I will gentle wash it and lay it flat on my dryer closet rack. It does have cotton content so it make shrink some, which won't hurt my feelings.  I will update if there are any issues.

I've never ordered a "3x" anything but I am used to buying 2 sizes larger for my top than my bottom area.  I'm 5'8" 38DD/DDD.  Sooooo... I ordered according to the sizing chart.  I actually really like the shirt.  You should know that the bottom does NOT have a tighter "band" area.  The photo sort of looks like it does.  It IS tighter at the waist.  I hits me just right, but I do not have a long torso.  The neck area is obviously a "scoop" neck.  It is a bit large.  It might slip a bit off the shoulder, but I'm ok with that look.  I would wear with a black bra and/or a black cami.  It is a good look for me.  I have a more hourglass shape.  If you are more of an "apple" shape, it might not be as comfortable.

I bought these for my grandson as my daughter loves "Vans"... and I wanted my grandson to begin to learn about tieing his shoes.  Most of his shoes have velcro closures or are slip ons.  Also, he loves The Blues Brothers (the edited version) and I bought him an "Elwood" costume (black suit, white shirt, skinny tie, sunglasses and fedora) and thought these rounded out the costume perfectly.  They are a wee bit large on him just yet so I expect he'll grow into them quickly.

Honestly, I bought this suit for my grandson to dress up like the Blues Brothers... he loves the car crashes.  In any case, it will be just fine for a costume. It is a machine washable polyester material and will last at least as long as he is interested.  My grandson is 3 years old and wears a 3 or 4 T.

I first bought this cleaner years ago on QVC.  I continue to buy it because it is safe for all metals and gems, including pearls.  It does a really good job cleaning.  I have a little container with a brush that I use with it.  I do recommend this cleaner!

I bought these with a matching thomas rain coat for my grandson to use while visiting me on the Oregon Coast.  They live in Arizona so rain coats and boots are not a part of daily life.  The boots are pretty big, but that's ok.  Better than too small.  He's going to love them.  Now, I just have to hope for some rain to make the puddles while they are here!

I bought this hat as part of a blues brothers costume for my grandson.  He's only 3 and the hat is little large, but that just adds to the cute factor really.  He loves the blues brothers (edited version of course) mostly for the car wreck scenes.  In any case, it's adorable. For the price, this was perfect.
I bought this as part of a blues brothers costume I am putting together for my grandson who is a big fan.  It's a bit long for him, but it's kind of cute that way.

Not much to say about these... I bought them for my 3 year old grandson to wear with boots while he's on the beach at my house.  They wash well and are thick.  I believe he will grow out of these before they wear out.

Ok... you get what you pay for.  And this case, not much.  I think it will work for the 1 week visit of my grandson, but probably little else.  The plastic is very thin and would tear very easily, but I am carefully hanging it until my grandson arrives.  If you're looking for a one time use, this is probably great.  I would spend few extra dollars for something beyond one use.

Bought these for hubby. He has had this "style" of Oakleys for a super long time.  We just keep replacing the lens.  However, during his last business trip, the frames and all went "missing."  He was devastated.  SO.  I bought these as replacement.  Might be a good idea to keep one set of frames as a back up.  On a happy note, the missing frames appeared again.  So now he has a backup pair.  These are the only sunglasses he uses.  He changes the lens per what he is doing.... yellow for night driving... dark for sun... etc. Also, should note, he has a pretty big head and these fit and stay fitting.  Hope this helps!

Looking at the photo I thought I was buying something a bit shorter, but the description says 60" so be sure that you realize that.  I needed to store a leather jacket with fox fur trimmed hood.  This will work, but really would have preferred a jacket length.

I really love these tops.  They would not works as "cover ups" for me, but I love them as tunics with jeggings.  This particular one has a little lower cinch and looks terrific with white jeggings.  Very summery.  The ties have plastic beads on them to help the ties hang properly, They wash wonderfully.  I do throw them into a delicate bag when washing to keep the ties from getting too wild.  I wear a 14/16, 38DD/DDD and am 5'8".  These tunics hit me high thigh, which I like.  The V is deep, but you could wear a cammy or even a tank if you'd like. Hope this helps!

I know this is supposed to be a beach cover, but I purchased it to wear with jeggings and travel.  It is large, but the waist cinch really pulls it in and looks nice and comfortable.  I wear a women's 14/16, 5'8" and 38DD/DDD.  The V is pretty deep, but one could wear a camisole with it, or let the cleavage be.  The lightweight poly material will be great for machine wash/tumble dry and pack very well.  I have purchased a couple of different cover ups from this seller and they are really wonderful tunics!  A bit too short for me to consider as a coverup. This one hits me high thigh.  Hope this info is useful!

I purchased this for pool side or spa treatment attire as I travel.  The poly material will do well traveling and is easy to put on and cinch for a flattering look.  I wear a women's 14/16, 5'8" and 38DD/DDD.  The kaftan falls about 3-4 inches above my ankle which I like.  I wear flats and don't like things that drag on the floor.  The poly material will do will as a swim coverup or as spa treatment attire because it's very easy to slip on and off.  Machine washed and dried beautifully.  The color is navy and white and the pattern is crisp and beautiful.  I think it was a good buy considering price/quailty.  With free returns, I will definitely check what else this seller has to offer.  In fact, I am going to look for this print in a tunic to wear with jeggings!  Perfect for summer travel.

I purchased this cover up to use as a tunic.  It's a bit too short for me to consider as a coverup, but PERFECT for a tunic.  I wear it with jeggings and for travel.  It's very lightweight material that is very sheer. And will likely do well packed in a suitcase. I plan to wear it with a nude or navy camisole (the color I purchased has blues and greens). I wear a women's 14/16, 5'8" and 38DD/DDD.  The V is a comfortable, no cleavage reveal.  The tie is slightly dropped, so it's not empire waisted and I think it is a very flattering look.  There is a very pretty scalloped edge cotton trim around the neck and plastic type beads weigh the ties perfectly.  I think it is a fair buy in price/quality.  With free returns, I decided to try it and am very, very pleased.  Will look at other things this seller has.

Ordered these for my husband and unfortunately he was out of the country and not able to try them on before the return possiblity expired. (1 month) They run very, very small for wide.  In fact, they are barely normal width.  I would not recommend this boot or this seller on return policy.

This bag is quite large and very sturdy.  I managed to pack it and NOT have to pay an over weight fee, but you could easily overfill it because there is so much room.  It's a tough canvas material and handles are sewn securely.  If you have to travel a lot and carry a bit with you, I would recommend it.

The attachment cords were difficult to use and I would opt for something I could do more quickly.. on the "up" side, your suitcase handle would probably give before these cords would.  Not sure that's a good thing.  Thus my rating.

I bought these through Amazon as a matter of convenience, but honestly you can get them a bit cheaper at most stores that carry travel items. They are TSA approved, meaning that you set the number code and if the TSA needs a look, they use a master key to access entry.  I rated 4 stars because pricing should be better, even through Amazon.

My husband was looking for something that really does work as carry on and yet, had a roller to save the back.  First you should know that this bag is very big for a "back pack" roller.  It has an expander zipper and could easily be overstuffed, making it difficult to put in overheads.  My husband travels 11 of 12 months of the year, including a lot of international flights.  He finds this bag to be very useful in multiple ways with lots of zippered pouches for travel documents, notepad, files, medications and some clothes.The main compartment opens like a suitcase, side to side, not from the top as you would with a smaller back pack. The rest of the zipper compartments open widely and from the top.  He highly recommends it for a frequent traveler.  I think it would be great for any guy.  I think it would be a bit too big for me though.  Hope this helps!

I was tired of trying to balance my luggage on my bathroom scale to see if I was within limits.  We do a lot of traveling using different bags, etc.  This really works well.  It appears to match what the airline scale says.  I would much prefer to "repack" bags at home if one bag is over than at the check in desk at the airport. You attach it to the handle, lift and hold until it beeps and then read the weight at the top of the handle.  I hope it will work for more than a few months.... will update if I have any issues.

Husband travels a lot with odd sized stuff and wanted something easy to see on the luggage mover.  Oh yeah.  These are REALLY bright.  Lightweight, but seem like they will be sturdy. Sizing is accurate.  Not too big, but not really small.  I will update if there are any issues.
The sizing was really off.  I would reccommend ordering at least one size or possibly two sizes up.  I probably won't order this style/brand again.

I purchased 2 of these, beige and black. They are very light weight, but that is good.  Even being light weight, they are still quite warm.  They are not scratchy (not wool) and a perfect length to double over.  The beige is a rich bone color.  The black is nice and dark. The price is great.  Overall, I would definitely consider other colors.

This is a very dainty, light necklace.  However, I think it is actually better than I expected.  I purchased it as a gift for someone I don't know terribly well.  I do believe she will really like it.  The cross is less than an inch in length and the chain is a very light weight, box type link. It's a little slinky.  I didn't measure the chain, but it looks standard 16-18".  I came in a black card board type box with a slit piece inside to hold the necklace in place.  It looks very nice with a ribbon wrapped around it - I added the ribbon.  All in all, I think it will be well received and I am proud to give it. Hope this helps!
I bought this for a friend and I wll fill it with things for Christmas.  It's very high quaility and looks more expensive that it was.  I think it will be very well received.

I have to say that I have collected these... one color at a time. I own the black, the chocolate and now these in chestnut. They are comfortable, stylish and easy. The sizing is spot on. I wear an 8 1/2 - 9 and the 9's are fine. The side buttons work, however you really don't need them. They pull on easily. I do recommend treating them so they are easier to clean and more waterproof. Especially the lighter ones like these in chestnut. The other thing to note is that they are light weight. The calves are easy to tuck your jeans into, but not too loose. Honestly, I pretty much wear only these for the winter. Rain is fine, but I don't wade through mud puddles.
Hope this is helpful!

I knew by reading previous reviews that this hoodie was thinner, not a thick fuzzy hoodie and that was perfect for me. I don't like the added bulk.  My disappointement is in the sleeve length.  They are too short for my taste.  However, I will keep it as a runaround.  Otherwise, the fit is what I expected from Woman Within. I am currently washing it.  I have a closet top dryer.  I will hang it in there on really super low heat and hope the sleeves don't shrink up any more!  Hope this helps!

These were purchased for a one-time, funny wear.  Good thing, because 1/2 of them were broken when they arrived.  If you're ok with a pretty poor overall quality (meaning: No more than one time use), these are probably ok.

I bought this before Halloween for my grandson.  As a family, we all wore a version of skeleton tshirts, including my daughter (his mommy) who is pregnant.  It was really cute.  The tshirt is of good quality, medium to heavy weight.  It has washed nicely and sizing appears to be spot on.

I bought these as a gag gift and that's about what they are worth... pretty chinzy.  But they were fairly cheap too.  So I'm not too surprised. Just know that you're not buying a great quailty pair of sunglasses and you're fine.

I bought this as a birthday gift for my college god daughter.  She reports that it is very useful and cool.  So I consider that a win/win!  There were many prints and options.  Also a reasonable price. It arrived on time and wrapped.  Thanks Amazon.

The blouse fits just as expected, no need to go up or down a size. The photo shows a fairly accurate view of the colors. The drawn string neck is NOT functional, it just ties at the "V"... does not cinch up at all. But that's ok because it is plenty high enough. It is light weight and not quite as see through as shown in the photos. I love that Lucky gives those of us who need a bit more room for the girls an option. I haven't laundered it yet but the directions and material will do fine in the "delicate/handwash" cycle of my machine.
*The blouse I received had apparently been tried on by someone else... Kind of bummer because it was kinda stinky.

When I opened the cello bag and unfolded the blouse, I found there were small rips on the side of the blouse.  I didn't even try it on.  I would return, but the cost to return it is almost the price of the blouse.  So I will just throw it away.  It's not even goot enough for a donation.  If you're determined to try it... go up about 2 sizes it appears to run very small. Also, the material is very, very thin and see through.  Apparently the material is also easily torn.

Husband likes to put on his boots quickly and he has wide feet.  He recently had to have orthotics for plantar facititis. (sp?) He does wish these boots were a tiny bit wider for the orthotics.  But even still, these are his "go to" boots.

This blouse is really cute on and the color is spot on.  It's a rich coral.  The sleeves are interesting and the fit is flattering. Sometimes this type of blouse can look a little "maternity"... but not this one.  It's well designed and made. Love it. FYI: machine washed and tumble dried with no trouble!

This blouse runs small and I ordered 2 sizes up. It worked wonderfully. I am 5'8", wear a 14/16 and 38DD/DDD. My body shape is a classic hour glass. The sleeves are snug, but not too tight and ordering up made the lower portion of the blouse a bit looser. Perfect. It's a fancy blouse that could be worn with some black slacks or a black pencil skirt.  It is very flattering on. The "v" neck is deep, but I don't think it's too deep.  One could wear a camisole underneath if you chose to.

These are my mothers FAV panties.  They are very hard to find elsewehere. I will say that Mom is not that big and we ordered the size she always prefers.  She doesn't like tight panties.  These are loose.  They wash and dry well and last a very long time.

I bought this hat as part of Christmas gift for my teenage niece. It's harder and harder to buy fun stuff for teens, but I really think she is going to have some great fun with this.
It is actually quite large. It looks and feels like a felt material. It appears to be well made. It is a very bright blue and I believe the photo is close to the actual color. Again, I think it's going to be quite the riot on Christmas morning.

I bought this t-shirt because it is sort of a family joke on me. I can handle snakes, mice, frogs, beetles, grasshoppers.. pretty much everything. But spiders.
I bought a super large one because I wanted it to be really baggy and it is. The jersey material is heavy and feels high quality. The logo print is centered and crisply done. I washed and dried it per instructions and it looks great. I expect to get years and years of smiles and wear out of it.
On shipping, it arrived as promised and in great condition.

BEFORE BUYING you should know:

1. They are in Thailand. If you have to return the item for any reason, it will cost YOU approximately $20. (Mine was $18.95 from Oregon) They (Koh Koh) will also ask you to declare the value of the return as $10.00 so they do not get charged tax for the item being returned. No where in the Amazon posting does it indicate these dresses are shipping from (and returned to) Thailand. It does says "FREE SHIPPING WORLDWIDE"... but that doesn't tell you anything really. I will say that it arrived relatively quickly. Too bad it was the dress was such a terrible mess. ** UPDATE** 9/25 After a long communication "battle" a full refund, including my shipping has been processed.

2. I actually really like the design of this dress. However, when the dress arrived (I ordered the red and white), the red part of the print had bled into the white and in some areas there were big red sploches on the white area of the graphic print. The bleed so bad that what should have been crisp, clean lines of the graphic design where "blurred" over the entire dress. I can't believe they actually shipped it to me in this condition. I would have liked to have ordered other colors (the navy/white and the black/white), but I will not risk they will be color bled or worse. What if it is a cleaning issue? Possibly a bad dye batch? Who knows? The dress I received should never have been sent out.

DRESS DESIGN INFO: I will say that I found the sizing to be acurate per their provided info. The material hangs well and SHOULD be machine wash/tumble or hang dry, although I am not able to atest to that as it was immediately returned. The dress material is very thin but not see thru. To be honest, since it was such a hot mess I did not really inspect the sewing quality. The dress (design and material hand) does not look like a million dollar dress, but it would work nicely for casual wear or casual evening wear in my opinion. I also believe the design of the dress is one that would be flattering on a wide range of women and it is nice they offer some larger sizes. The red is sort of a brick and the photo is fairly accurate as to the color, and the white was a little more ivory/pink, but that is probably due to the ink bleed of the red onto the white. I did not find the "V" neck too low for me, but it would be possible to wear a cami under for modesty.

I strive to provide honest and geniunely helpful reviews each time I do so. I hope it has been helpful to provide this info.

My husband has a grand collection of VERY expensive watches and THIS is the watch he wears most of the time. In fact, this is the third time I've purchased this exact watch over the years.  Eventually the velco band gives way.  And he wants another of the same.  At around $20, it's a steal.  It's remarkably tough as he wears it while working in his shop.  Obviously I would recommend it as this is the third time I've purchased it for him.  :)

These are cotton canvas bags.  I got them to decorate as party favor bags.  They are a bit smaller than I would have liked, but I believe the dimensions provided were close. I was just hoping for slightly more room.  They did smell a bit musty.  I aired them out and gave them a quick spray of Febreeze.

It's been a long time since I've had a front loader... but this one fits me perfectly. Keeps the girls up. Just the right about lining (light foam forms and smooths and helps to hide the "headlights"). I think one of the best things about this bra is that it is VERY comfortable! Honestly, I've got several of these. I'm picky and tough to fit. This bra really works.
UPDATE.... 6/2016.... I am STILL buying this bra. Honestly I wish they would make a 38DDD... but I make the 38DD work. One thing you should know is these are front loaders... so this means that you will not be able to adjust the body once it has been worn and "relaxes"... I can usually get about 18 months out of one... I use a lingerie bag to wash on the "hand wash" cycle in my machine.

I love love love this Torrid bra. I sure wish they would do a true "nude".... this one is actually PINK with a nude lace overlay... pretty, but predominately PINK, not nude. Dang it. Would have done 5 stars if it was really "nude"... all that said, this bra is fully lined and padded to give the girls the BEST lift. The great thing is that it LIFTS the girls and doesn't push them under you armpits. Hate that with a minimizers... (this bra is NOT a minimizer). It looks great under clothes and does a great job lifting. It's super comfortable for hours and hours. Additional bonus: the padding also protects the dreaded headlights. I find the sizing to be true. Just be prepared for the amazing, youthful look you're going to achieve with this bra!
Torrid, if you're listening.... a TRUE NUDE please!! The black is great, the white is true white. Lose the TOO pink back to the nude!! :-)

I bought these to help at Mom's garage sale... it was super easy to tell people to "pay a person with a red apron!" The pockets were great to hold a note pad and some spare change.  They washed wonderfully and we'll use them again!

I bought this wrap to go with a pasley printed dress that is predominately turquoise and it's a perfect match. Yes, it fits snuggly, but it's yoga wear. It's not suppose to be loosy goosey. But everyone is different. I would give prAna five stars if they offered Plus sizes. It is perfect for the use I purchased for, but it could be more useful in a wider range of sizes!
I have a larger bust (38DDD) and I fits over the top of the girls, and knots alittle lower than empire waist area, quite a lot higher than the photo shows, so beware of that. The sleeves are very snug, but I like that too. Holds me in a little. So that works for me. The sleeves are extra long... another thing I like.

I bought this bag as a creative way to "wrap" a gift for my sister-in-law in. I was quite surprised at the quality of the bag. There is a zippered pocket inside and it is lined with a poly (easy to clean) type lining.  She is going to love the bag and I am very proud to give it to her.

I HATE doing bare feet at the airport... YUCK.  So I bought these at less than a dollar a pair.  I COULD bag and wash them, but honestly, about 1/2 the time I toss them.  I'd buy the paper booties, but they are more expensive than a pair of socks.
I had to return the top I bought to go with these bottoms... As much as I loved, loved, loved the color...

My Mom had a red wool pair of these she's had for so many years there were holes in the toe!  She only wears them in the house... anyway, when I found these in her favorite color purple I rolled the dice and sent them to her.  She retired the old worn pair and was so thrilled.  I'm sure these will last for years too.
We were on Maui for Christmas and of course needed the Christmas Aloha shirt... You will need to buy one size up for that looser, more comfortable fit.  Washes and wears like a dream.

I have to say this one of my favorite shirts my husband wears while were are on the islands.  It fits well, washes well and requires little pressing if you hang it right away.  It's just a cool shirt.  I also recommend sizing up one size for a looser fit.

If you've got a larger chest, this style is the perfect thing.  I am a 38DDD but found the best fit in their 38E or F..  I just love that it is an option for a larger cup!  This style is so flattering with a hidden underwire and yet, comfortable!  A hard thing to do.  Swimsuits are so personal, but I would highly recommend you try this top if you're girls are larger!  I've bought several different colors and tankinis from this seller.  I am devoted buyer!

This is my first and probably last purchase from this vendor, Carol Wright Gifts. There are a couple of things to note about this jumper. First, as other reviewers note, DO ORDER AT LEAST ONE SIZE UP.  Glad I did.  I am not a huge person, but my bust is. Secondly, the material is more of a "velour" terry, in case that matters to you. It is softer than full cotton terry, but thinner.  AND there is only ONE pocket, on the right hand side, so lefty's should know.  Why wouldn't they do two pockets?? Obviously to save money, but I probably would not have purchased it if that had been properly noted in the product description. In fact, pockets are not even mentioned in the outline. I "assumed" there were two pockets with the model putting her hand in one... I considered returning them, but honestly the return postage would be more than I paid for the jumper, so I will be not returning. Also, they are NOT kidding when they say it will take F O R E V E R  to get to you after purchase... I honestly didn't believe it would take that long. Believe it.  While it arrived as estimated, holy cow it was a LONG time. If you're in a hurry, look at some other vendor.  I will probably not consider things from this vendor again due to the low quality and slow shipping. I can only imagine the refund process.

I bought this for a quick-to-pull-on lounger in the morning while I enjoy coffee on the lanai...  The material is soft, silky and thin, as rayon is.  It does require a gentle hand wash and a line dry... The "sparkles" they reference is actually sparkle glue, not beads.  I handwashed and the sparkle glue remained intact. It would also work comfortably as a swimsuit coverup.  The tie that at the waist is alittle chinzy.. wish they would have used the same rayon material for the tie instead of a cheap cotton, but it works.. would have been 5 stars with a better tie. With the tie, the style lends it's self to many different body sizes without looking like a sack.  I am very happy with it and will consider possible other colors!
These are were a little loose on my head... but they are very cool and a little retro, although a little too large for true retro.

These look soooooo cool on!! One word of advice, it is necessary to ORDER ONE FULL SIZE UP. They run very short. Other that, these are my most favorite flip flops (or as in my generation, we called these "thongs"). I also will say that even though these are a rubber/rubber like material they do not rub the area between my toes raw as the ones of my youth did. They are comfortable and very stylish. Love them! I ordered these in the black/silver and with that, I don't need any others!
UPDATE 7/2014.... STILL my favs. I've ordered several "backup pairs" for when my originals eventually wear out, gold and silver. I keep 2 pairs for travel too. I love love these... but do remember to go up one size for length!!

For color, I would it give 5 stars...AWESOME with a tan. It is a darker coral than shown in the photos... more like a neon coral. But I like that. AND let's all support MADE IN THE USA!!! However, I continue to struggle, trying to figure out is Ujena sizing... I have ordered several different suits... Reading other reviews, I ordered 2 sizes bigger on top, to achieve that looser look shown on the model.. I am a 38DD and the 3x fits in the bust area nicely, however the bottom portion of the top is about 4 -5 inches too long, more like a form fitting swimdress, not a tankini.  The inner lining, meant to be a miminizer, is almost as loose as the outer top, so I wouldn't count on that to help hold you in.  I don't need it so I will probably remove it and possibly hem the top. Another note, if you're super modest, you should know the bust portion, while lined, is thin. "Nips" are visible. As for the bottom, I normally order a 1x however with Ujena I have learned the bottoms need about 1 size up...usually, however, these are so large I couldn't wear them in a pool without a "wardrobe malfunction." Plus, the little ties on the side are cute and are functional to scrunch the sides. They draw attention to your thighs. So if your confident in that area, go for it.  I am ordering a different style bottom as I am not really thrilled with my thighs.  :-)  Listen, no woman's body is exactly the same, and so, I will continue to shop Ujena because I can buy the larger top and smaller bottom.  One day, I will figure out the mystery of their sizing and I will share it!!

This aloha shirt is my husband's "go to" shirt while we are on the islands... It is comfortable and stylish (not his highest concern - but I like it too)... It is a alittle stiff until you wash it a few times, but the quality will last for a long time with care.  My guy is a "big" guy... just a note on the sizing... always go up one size.  For some reason, all the aloha shirts are like this.  Plus it gives them a little button room... All in all, it's one of my favorites on him!

Ok... These are nice slippers with rubber bottoms which is good if you have to run outside for anything... the BIG downside is that they run REALLY small.  So much so I would recommend ordering TWO sizes up... unless you like your heel riding the rise on the back/heel of the slipper.  Isotoner really wrote the book on comfort and slippers... a good brand... however they really need to consider WHERE they are having the slippers made.  Slippers SHOULD run a little large because that is how slippers are worn!

This blouse is TERRIFIC. Especially if you have "big girls".... It's stretchy. (I am a 38DDD) It's super comfortable.  The center cinching is adjustable for a deep plunge, or more conservative.  Be sure to follow their specific sizing, you will NOT be disappointed.  I am considering other colors this such a good, figure-flattering design. I'm taking this to Maui with me. One thing I will say... it is NOT black... It's more of a gray/green.  Which I do not mind.  But to be honest, it is NOT black and white... in case that matters to you!

I gave the swimsuit 3 stars for the "hold you in" part... BUT... the way the skirt and waist are sewn, when you have it on, it looks like the skirt is longer on one hip than the other... It's an optical illision, but it is... awkward looking.  That's the best way I could describe it.  If you are one of those tiny size 2 people, it may be less awkward.  I also found that the deep dip in the back was not flattering if you have ANY back fat, as most humans over 30 do. Listen, at $160, I expect the suit to fit well and look well made.  All in all, I was disappointed and am returning the suit.

There wasn't any specific sizing info on these shorts, but they run large and very baggy in the rear and legs and seemed extra long for my taste. I am NOT a size 8 but these made me look like I was a size 26.  The material was light and comfortable.  They were just really unflattering on me.  I am about 5'9" and a women's 14/16.
The swim top is not as sheer as represented ...but maybe that is a good thing. It is also longer than. I expected...but again probably a good thing. Looks less "maternity ".

I made it a purpose to review these brushes. I usually get a brush from the dollar store, or a cheap one at Walmarts. These brushes average $2 a piece. I can't even begin to tell you what a difference these brushes make in washing dishes. My old brushes turned the color of whatever food you were trying to clean off. You had to use pressure, scrub, then run your brush through the dishwasher along with the dish. These brushes clean so well, and without residue that I've just done them by hand many times. It's absolutely insane the difference it makes.  The brush stays clean and you don't need to use much pressure. I'll never buy another cheap brush again. I enclosed photos wearing Brooks gts adrenaline 21 gts on the left prosthesis, reebok royal nylon on the right foot, and an Athletic Work (very inexpensive shoe from Walmart) that I wear around the house.
The Brooks gts 21 are the most roomy and comfortable. The Reebok Royal Nylon are a bit shorter and much narrower than the others; so narrow that my forefoot began to hurt after wearing them.

I am normally a 16/18 and size XL. I'm pregnant and wanted to find some alternatives to maternity leggings so decided to just size up to a 2X to test them out. I'm still pretty early and haven't changed much in the stomach area and they were close to snug in the waistline, and the legs were oddly baggy in spots. Which never happens with me, I have pretty sturdy legs and never have issues with leggings being baggy on me.

The most ridiculous part of these pants though is that they have these mesh cutouts in strange spots, like a long triangular section right on the hip that starts about mid-top of the thigh and goes clear up to the waistband. So you either need to wear black underwear with it or none at all, because it will absolutely show through.

The fabric didn't seem overly stretched out or anything. I got this on sale for around $15 I believe and at that price, I am happy with it. I am not sure that I would willingly spend more than about $20-25 max on it though. The black is nice, the fabric is on the thinner side but isn't terrible and it doesn't look "cheap", but I don't know if I would try my luck with any of the prints though.

If you are right between sizes, or are tall, have long arms, bigger chest, etc... then I would consider sizing up if possible. But I think you might also be ok with getting your normal size, just depends on your build really.
